title: SPM packaging: :pack-generated Package.swift from barekit-pin.json + min versions recorded
description:
CONTEXT: Standing decision (CEO row 14, single pin source): Package.swift is GENERATED by dart run flutter_pear:pack from barekit-pin.json — never hand-edit. The Swift-host task hand-seeded packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/flutter_pear_bare/Package.swift; this task replaces that with deterministic generation. Background (Eng2, CEO row 15): upstream bare-kit releases ship ONE ~354MB multi-platform prebuilds.zip that SPM URL binaryTargets cannot consume, so the pack epic added a maintainer repack step and barekit-pin.json carries version + URL + a two-link checksum chain (upstream sha256 -> repacked sha256). The generated Package.swift must contain: path binaryTargets for every committed packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/addons/*.xcframework, a binaryTarget(name:url:checksum:) for the repacked BareKit whose checksum equals the pin's repacked sha256, and platforms set to BareKit's prebuild minimum iOS version (from the pin manifest). Layout reference: docs.flutter.dev packages-and-plugins/swift-package-manager/for-plugin-authors. Files: packages/flutter_pear/bin/pack.dart (the :pack tool — buildBundle near line 258, linkNativeAddons near line 103); barekit-pin.json (pack-epic artifact — locate with find from the repo root; if absent, stop and flag with bd human: cross-epic prerequisite not met); COMPATIBILITY.md at the repo root (append-only contract file checked by packages/flutter_pear/bin/check_compatibility.dart; the pin-consistency checker itself is the tests epic, not this task).
DO:
1. Add a generatePackageSwift step to packages/flutter_pear/bin/pack.dart, invoked from main after linkNativeAddons: read barekit-pin.json, enumerate committed ios/addons/*.xcframework in sorted order, and write Package.swift from a fixed template with a header comment: GENERATED BY dart run flutter_pear:pack — DO NOT EDIT. Output must be byte-identical across runs given unchanged inputs.
2. Run :pack so the working-tree Package.swift is the generated one (leave uncommitted).
3. Record the minimum Flutter version for the SPM consumer path and the iOS deployment target in COMPATIBILITY.md (new v0.2 row area, following the file's append-only note). The compat podspec task records the same iOS minimum in the podspec; if the podspec already exists, keep the two values identical.
4. Zero-manual-edit consumer proof on the example app: flutter clean then a sim build with no Xcode interaction.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Package.swift is generated, carries the DO NOT EDIT header, and is byte-identical across two consecutive :pack runs.
- Its URL binaryTarget checksum equals barekit-pin.json's repacked sha256.
- Its path binaryTargets exactly match the committed ios/addons/*.xcframework set.
- Minimum Flutter (SPM path) and iOS deployment target recorded in COMPATIBILITY.md.
- Example app builds for the iOS simulator via SPM with zero manual Xcode edits.
AUTO-VALIDATION:
- cd packages/flutter_pear and run: dart run flutter_pear:pack twice; after the second run, git diff --quiet on ../flutter_pear_bare/ios/flutter_pear_bare/Package.swift must exit 0 (deterministic).
- python3 script (write it under the scratchpad) that parses the checksum out of Package.swift and the repacked sha256 out of barekit-pin.json and prints MATCH only when equal — must print MATCH.
- cd packages/flutter_pear_example and run: flutter clean, then flutter build ios --simulator --debug — succeeds; ls build/ios/Debug-iphonesimulator/Runner.app/Frameworks piped to grep -i bare shows the BareKit framework embedded.
REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).
3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.
CONSTRAINTS: committed artifacts (bundle, jniLibs, xcframeworks, generated Package.swift) are versioned like source and a fresh checkout must never need :pack to build; new dependencies must be MIT/Apache-2.0/BSD/ISC; do not relitigate standing v0.2 decisions (arm64-only sim slices, committed addons, single flutter_pear_bare package).
notes:
IMPLEMENTED: generatePackageSwift in pack.dart now writes the REAL Flutter SPM plugin manifest at packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/flutter_pear_bare/Package.swift (packageSwiftRelativePath updated), replacing the flutter_pear-ovt.3.1 hand-seeded file with byte-for-byte the same structure: hyphenated library product name (flutter-pear-bare, Flutter's own SPM-plugin naming convention), a FlutterFramework path dependency, the CBareKit shim target (BareKit.xcframework ships no module map), the flutter_pear_bare Swift target depending on CBareKit plus every addon target (sorted), and binaryTargets for BareKit (url/checksum from barekit-pin.json) plus every committed ios/addons/*.xcframework. title: iOS lifecycle per D11: native suspendWithLinger honoring Dart linger + state diagram in BOTH hosts
description:
CONTEXT: The spike epic recorded the D11 lifecycle decision — evidence of what iOS actually does to the Dart-side linger timer when the app backgrounds (iOS may freeze the Dart isolate before the timer fires). Read it FIRST: bd list/bd show on the spike epic's issues (search bd for D11 or lifecycle). Dart today: packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/lifecycle.dart — PearLifecycle wires AppLifecycleState to suspend/resume; PearLifecycleDefaults.linger = Duration(seconds: 20) at line 28 is Android-tuned; Pear (packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/pear.dart) constructs it in Pear._. Hosts: packages/flutter_pear_bare/android/src/main/kotlin/tech/loable/flutter_pear_bare/FlutterPearBarePlugin.kt and packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/flutter_pear_bare/Sources/flutter_pear_bare/FlutterPearBarePlugin.swift (from the Swift-host task). Standing decision DX2-52: if native-side suspend lands, it MUST honor the Dart-configured PearLifecycle(linger:) value — a public API must never be silently bypassed. Standing decision eng-4A: the worklet-lifecycle ASCII state diagram goes into BOTH native hosts as a header comment, added to Kotlin in the same change, so the hosts cannot drift silently. BareKit's suspend-with-linger API is in holepunchto/bare-kit v2.3.0; react-native-bare-kit ios/ shows the exact selector usage.
DO:
1. Read the D11 record and quote it in this issue's notes. Branch A (native suspend needed): in the Swift host observe UIApplication.didEnterBackgroundNotification; inside a short UIApplication beginBackgroundTask call the BareKit worklet suspend-with-linger using the Dart-configured linger, ending the background task afterward; resume on willEnterForegroundNotification. Plumb the value: add an optional lingerMs int entry to the start method-channel argument map — BareWorklet.start in packages/flutter_pear_bare/lib/src/bare_worklet.dart forwards it, Pear.start passes its lifecycle.linger in milliseconds; the Android host ignores the key (Dart timer stays authoritative there). Document the new parameter on every public symbol touched.
2. Branch B (D11 says the Dart-side path suffices): no native observer; document the iOS linger reality in PearLifecycle's dartdoc instead.
3. Either branch: write ONE ASCII state diagram (stopped -> running -> suspended -> running -> stopped, plus reattach and crash edges, matching WorkletState in bare_worklet.dart) and paste it as a header comment in BOTH FlutterPearBarePlugin.kt and FlutterPearBarePlugin.swift, each marked as a mirrored comment that must be edited in both files together.
4. Branch A only: extend packages/flutter_pear_bare/test/bare_worklet_test.dart to assert the start payload carries lingerMs when supplied (it already mocks the flutter_pear_bare/control channel).
5. Sim check script or documented command sequence: launch the example on a booted sim, background it with xcrun simctl openurl booted https://example.com, wait past the linger, relaunch with xcrun simctl launch booted BUNDLEID, and grep the flutter run log for a suspend line followed by a resume line with the app still responsive.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- D11 decision quoted in issue notes; implemented branch matches it exactly.
- Branch A: native suspend uses the Dart-configured linger; a custom linger provably reaches the start payload (unit test); Android behavior unchanged.
- Both hosts carry the identical ASCII lifecycle diagram header comment.
- Suspend/resume code path passes on the iOS simulator (state transitions observed in logs; the sim validates the code path only, not real background socket-kill — that caveat is documented elsewhere, do not claim more).
AUTO-VALIDATION:
- cd packages/flutter_pear_bare and run: flutter test — passes. Mutation (Branch A): stop forwarding lingerMs in bare_worklet.dart; the new assertion must fail; revert.
- The DO step 5 sim sequence, with grep on the run log showing suspend then resume.
REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).
3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.
CONSTRAINTS: Futures for calls, broadcast Streams for events, no callbacks; every public symbol documented; do not relitigate the standing v0.2 decisions (D11 is decided by the spike's evidence — implement it, do not re-investigate; sim-tier validation stands).
notes:
D11-DECISION (quoted verbatim from flutter_pear-ovt.1.9's close reason): 'D11 decided from evidence: UNCLEAN. Dart-side linger Timer freezes entirely while the app is backgrounded on the iOS simulator and only catches up (firing onSuspend immediately before onResume) the instant the app returns to foreground -- confirmed across 3 rounds with background durations of 23s/37s/65s, all identical. The worklet is never actually suspended during real backgrounding today; native-side suspendWithLinger in the future Swift host (honoring the Dart linger value, DX2 #52) is the recorded fix, deferred to the E3 host epic.' Branch A (native suspend) implemented, matching this exactly.

IMPLEMENTED: bare_worklet.dart's BareWorklet.start gained an optional lingerMs int param, included in the start channel call's argument map only when non-null. pear.dart's Pear.start gained Duration linger = PearLifecycleDefaults.linger, forwarded as lingerMs to BOTH BareWorklet.start call sites (initial + kill-restart retry) and to Pear._'s constructor (which now takes linger and passes it to PearLifecycle(linger:) -- this also closes a real pre-existing gap: Pear.start previously had NO way at all for a caller to customize the linger DX2 #52 requires be honorable, since PearLifecycle was always constructed with the hardcoded default).

FlutterPearBarePlugin.swift: added static lingerMs storage (updated on every start() call that supplies one, including reattaches) and a one-time (guarded by backgroundObserversRegistered) registration of didEnterBackgroundNotification/willEnterForegroundNotification observers in register(with:) -- registered once for the process lifetime since they touch only static state, so a later hot-restart-created plugin instance never double-registers them. On background: begins a short UIApplication background task, calls worklet.suspend(withLinger:) with the configured (or 20000ms default) value, ends the task immediately after (matches DO step 1's own 'short... ending afterward' wording, not held open for the full linger). On foreground: calls worklet.resume(). Added NSLog lines on both transitions (entered background / entering foreground) since native NSLog doesn't appear in flutter run's own console at all -- discovered this the hard way during AUTO-VALIDATION (see below) -- these are real operational log lines, not throwaway instrumentation, kept permanently.

ASCII lifecycle diagram (stopped/running/suspended + reattach + crash edges, matching WorkletState) added as a header comment to BOTH FlutterPearBarePlugin.kt and FlutterPearBarePlugin.swift -- verified programmatically byte-identical between the two files (first draft used self-referential wording per file while claiming VERBATIM, caught and fixed to neutral iOS-host/Android-host phrasing so the claim is actually true).

AUTO-VALIDATION, run for real:
1. flutter test (flutter_pear_bare) -- 20 passed, including 2 new bare_worklet_test.dart tests (lingerMs forwarded when supplied; omitted -- not sent as null -- when not supplied). Mutation spot-check: removed the lingerMs forwarding line in bare_worklet.dart -- both new tests failed as expected (plus 2 cascading failures from the mutation-broken test not reaching its own terminate() call and leaking worklet state into later tests in the same run -- a pre-existing test-isolation characteristic, not something this change introduced); reverted, all 20 passed again.
2. flutter test (flutter_pear) -- 248 passed, unaffected by the Pear.start/Pear._ signature change.
3. Real simulator check (DO step 5): flutter build ios --simulator --debug succeeded (temporary local BareKit override, same as every other 3.x task, reverted after). Ran flutter run with the flutter_pear-ovt.3.2 auto-join dart-define hook, backgrounded the app via xcrun simctl openurl, waited 25s (past the 20s default linger), relaunched via xcrun simctl launch. First attempt found NOTHING in flutter run's own log -- native NSLog output does not route through Flutter's console at all, a real gotcha this task's DO step 5 wording didn't anticipate. Re-ran capturing xcrun simctl spawn ... log stream in parallel instead (the same alternative flutter_pear-ovt.1.9's own DO steps already listed) and got clean real evidence: 12:47:36.966 entered background, arming suspendWithLinger(20000ms) -- 12:48:01.401 (about 24.4s later, past the linger) entering foreground, resuming worklet -- and the app relaunched and kept logging correctly afterward, confirming it was still responsive, not crashed or wedged.

melos run analyze: clean, 0 issues, all 4 packages.
close_reason: D11's native-suspend branch implemented: iOS host arms BareKit's suspendWithLinger on backgrounding using the Dart-configured PearLifecycle(linger:) value (newly threaded end-to-end through Pear.start, closing a real pre-existing gap where that value had no way to reach anywhere at all), verified for real on-simulator via a native log stream capture showing background-arm then foreground-resume across the linger window. Identical ASCII lifecycle diagram landed in both native hosts, verified byte-identical.

title: iOS storage roots: Application Support base + iCloud-backup exclusion on pear-corestore/pear-bulk
description:
CONTEXT: CRITICAL security/protocol correctness (Eng2 review, decision 35): on iOS the worklet storage base must be Application Support, NEVER Documents — an iCloud backup restore of Hypercore writer keys onto a second device FORKS cores (protocol corruption, not a UX nicety). The pear-end reads its storage root from Bare.argv[0] (packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/index.js line ~255 refuses to boot without it) and uses two subdirs: pear-corestore (line ~267) and pear-bulk (BULK_STORAGE_DIR, line ~262). Android passes context.filesDir (packages/flutter_pear_bare/android/src/main/kotlin/tech/loable/flutter_pear_bare/FlutterPearBarePlugin.kt line ~203) and excludes those dirs from backup via packages/flutter_pear_bare/android/src/main/res/xml/flutter_pear_data_extraction_rules.xml + flutter_pear_full_backup_content.xml, kept honest by packages/flutter_pear_bare/test/backup_rules_test.dart (asserts the config names match index.js's real dir names). The Swift host is packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/flutter_pear_bare/Sources/flutter_pear_bare/FlutterPearBarePlugin.swift (created by the Swift-host task). Files-app-visible output is a SEPARATE Documents subtree owned by the app/demo layer — the worklet never writes there; this task only guarantees the worklet root.
DO:
1. In the Swift host's fresh-boot path compute base = the user-domain applicationSupportDirectory URL + /flutter_pear; create it (Application Support is not auto-created on iOS), then eagerly create base/pear-corestore and base/pear-bulk BEFORE the worklet boots (index.js creates them lazily; the exclusion flag needs existing dirs).
2. Set isExcludedFromBackup = true via URLResourceValues on BOTH subdirs on every start (idempotent — a restore can resurrect dirs without the flag).
3. Pass base.path as argv[0] in the worklet start call.
4. Add a comment mirroring backup_rules_test.dart's honesty rule: these two dir names must match index.js; if the JS names change, both platforms' exclusions change together.
5. Add packages/flutter_pear_bare/test/ios_storage_rules_test.dart (TO BE CREATED, next to backup_rules_test.dart, same pattern — pure file-content Dart test so it runs anywhere): reads the Swift source and asserts it contains pear-corestore, pear-bulk, applicationSupportDirectory, and isExcludedFromBackup, and does NOT contain documentDirectory.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Worklet storage root on iOS is under Library/Application Support/flutter_pear; nothing worklet-owned lands in Documents.
- pear-corestore and pear-bulk exist after first Pear.start and carry the backup-exclusion attribute (verifiable on the sim filesystem).
- Drift-guard test passes, and fails if the Swift dir names or the Documents rule regress.
AUTO-VALIDATION:
- cd packages/flutter_pear_bare and run: flutter test test/ios_storage_rules_test.dart — passes. Mutation spot-check: change pear-corestore to pear-corestorex in FlutterPearBarePlugin.swift; the test must fail; revert.
- Run the example on a booted sim long enough for Pear.start (cd packages/flutter_pear_example; flutter test integration_test/ipc_transport_test.dart -d SIMID). Then, with APPDATA set to the output of xcrun simctl get_app_container booted BUNDLEID data (BUNDLEID from ios/Runner.xcodeproj PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER): ls of APPDATA/Library/Application Support/flutter_pear shows pear-corestore and pear-bulk; xattr on each of those two dirs prints com.apple.metadata:com_apple_backup_excludeItem; and test ! -e APPDATA/Documents/pear-corestore succeeds.
REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).
3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.
CONSTRAINTS: all P2P logic stays in JavaScript inside the Bare worklet and Dart is a typed remote control (the storage seam is argv[0] only — do not add new storage RPCs); do not relitigate standing v0.2 decisions (storage roots are deliberately non-configurable; sim-tier validation).
notes:
IMPLEMENTED and validated for real on the iOS simulator. FlutterPearBarePlugin.swift's startWorklet() now computes storageBase = Application Support/flutter_pear, with corestoreDir/bulkDir = storageBase/pear-corestore and storageBase/pear-bulk (names read directly from pear-end/index.js in the new drift-guard test, not hardcoded twice). Fresh boot: eagerly creates both dirs (withIntermediateDirectories), then applies isExcludedFromBackup=true to both via URLResourceValues, THEN passes storageBase.path as argv[0]. Reattach branch: re-applies the exclusion flag to both dirs on every start (idempotent, try? so a redundant re-assertion failure never blocks a reattach) per DO step 2's explicit every-start requirement. Added excludeFromBackup(_:) helper.

New test: packages/flutter_pear_bare/test/ios_storage_rules_test.dart (the iOS counterpart of backup_rules_test.dart) -- reads the Swift source and pear-end/index.js, asserts the exact quoted directory names appear, plus applicationSupportDirectory and isExcludedFromBackup are present and documentDirectory is absent.

AUTO-VALIDATION, run for real:
1. flutter test test/ios_storage_rules_test.dart -- passed. Mutation spot-check: renamed pear-corestore to pear-corestorex in the Swift file -- FIRST version of the test used a plain text.contains(corestoreDir) and gave a FALSE PASS (pear-corestorex still contains pear-corestore as a substring) -- caught this via the mutation check itself, fixed the test to require the closing quote right after the name (text.contains('"$corestoreDir"')), re-ran against the same mutation and confirmed it now correctly fails, then reverted the mutation and confirmed a clean pass again.
2. Real filesystem check on the booted iPhone 17 Pro (iOS 26.5) simulator: flutter run (not flutter test integration_test -- that ephemeral test runner uninstalls the app immediately after, leaving nothing to inspect) with the flutter_pear-ovt.3.2 auto-join dart-define hook, waited for reattached=false (fresh boot), then inspected the real container via xcrun simctl get_app_container ... data: Library/Application Support/flutter_pear/pear-corestore and .../pear-bulk both exist; xattr on both prints com.apple.metadata:com_apple_backup_excludeItem; Documents/ is empty (no pear-corestore, confirmed via test ! -e).

Required the same temporary local path-based BareKit.xcframework override as flutter_pear-ovt.3.1/3.2 for every validation run (real repackedUrl still PENDING-UPLOAD) -- reverted after every run, confirmed clean via git status each time.

melos run analyze: clean, 0 issues, all 4 packages. flutter_pear_bare full suite: 18 passed (17 previous + the new test).
close_reason: Worklet storage hardened to Application Support/flutter_pear with pear-corestore/pear-bulk excluded from iCloud backup, verified for real on-simulator (directories exist, correct xattr, nothing under Documents). New drift-guard test ios_storage_rules_test.dart passes with a genuine mutation spot-check (which also caught and fixed a substring-match false-pass bug in the test itself before it landed).

title: iOS simulator gate: hot-restart reattach-or-kill + handshake verified end-to-end
description:
CONTEXT: The Dart side already implements reattach-or-kill (E6.3 + flutter_pear-3vh): packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/pear.dart lines ~86-140 (Pear.start probes attach.info with the short PearRpcDefaults.attachHealthTimeout when BareWorklet.reattached is true; kills + reboots once on version mismatch or unhealthy reattach) and packages/flutter_pear_bare/lib/src/bare_worklet.dart (generationId stale-exit filtering, lines ~98-223). The Swift host (built in the Swift-host task at packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/flutter_pear_bare/Sources/flutter_pear_bare/FlutterPearBarePlugin.swift) mirrors the Kotlin generation machinery — but iOS has zero automated coverage of it. This task proves the loop on the iOS simulator (never require an app reinstall) and fixes any gap found. Hot restart is scriptable: flutter run supports --pid-file, and signaling that pid with SIGUSR2 triggers a hot restart (Flutter tooling contract). Example app: packages/flutter_pear_example (ios/ runner hydrated by the Swift-host task). A desktop peer for traffic exists at packages/flutter_pear_example/bin/peer.dart.
DO:
1. packages/flutter_pear_example/lib/main.dart does not currently print reattach status — add one debugPrint after Pear.start of the form: flutter_pear example: reattached= followed by pear.worklet.reattached, so the gate is greppable.
2. Add packages/flutter_pear_example/tool/ios_hot_restart_gate.sh (TO BE CREATED): boots/uses a sim; runs flutter run -d SIMID --pid-file under the scratchpad with output teed to a log; waits for the first reattached=false line; sends SIGUSR2 to the pid read from the pid file; waits for a subsequent reattached=true line (a reattached=false second line is acceptable ONLY if the log shows Pear.start's kill+fresh-boot path fired first); asserts the log contains no worklet_start_failed and no PearErrorCode.bundleVersionMismatch surfacing as a failure; then quits flutter run.
3. In the same script, after the restart: background the app with xcrun simctl openurl booted https://example.com, wait 5s, relaunch with xcrun simctl launch booted BUNDLEID (read PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER from packages/flutter_pear_example/ios/Runner.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj), and assert the app process still responds (a fresh log line after relaunch).
4. Fix any Swift-host gap the gate exposes (stale-generation onWorkletExit reaching a new generation, double-armed relay, etc.) by mirroring the corresponding Kotlin comment/behavior.
5. MANUAL: hot-restart during a message burst (Eng2 host-contract addition — the generation/stale-exit machinery has no iOS automated coverage): start a chat exchange against bin/peer.dart, hot restart mid-burst, confirm no crash and a working session after; note the evidence in this issue.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Scripted gate passes: hot restart reattaches (or cleanly kills+reboots) with no reinstall, no worklet_start_failed, handshake passes after restart.
- App still responds after a background/foreground cycle following the hot restart.
- ios_hot_restart_gate.sh is committed and runnable end-to-end.
- MANUAL: hot-restart-during-message-burst checked once; evidence recorded in issue notes.
AUTO-VALIDATION:
- bash packages/flutter_pear_example/tool/ios_hot_restart_gate.sh — exits 0 and prints the matched reattach lines.
REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).
3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.
CONSTRAINTS: all P2P logic stays in JavaScript inside the Bare worklet and Dart is a typed remote control; do not relitigate the standing v0.2 decisions (sim-tier validation is the accepted basis — do not demand physical-iPhone evidence).
notes:
IMPLEMENTED and validated for real on the iOS simulator (iPhone 17 Pro, iOS 26.5). Files: lib/main.dart gained a --dart-define gate hook (_gateAutoJoinTopic, read via String.fromEnvironment) that skips straight to ChatScreen and auto-joins a topic on launch when set (empty/default for every real run, zero behavior change) plus a debugPrint of reattached= after Pear.start in _join -- this is what makes the app driveable and observable from a headless shell script with no UI automation framework. tool/ios_hot_restart_gate.sh (new, executable): picks a booted sim or boots one, launches flutter run --pid-file with the gate define, waits for the first reattached=false line, sends SIGUSR2, waits for reattached=true, asserts no worklet_start_failed/BUNDLE_VERSION_MISMATCH, then backgrounds the app via simctl openurl, relaunches via simctl launch (bundle id read from project.pbxproj), and confirms the debug connection still responds via a SIGUSR1 hot reload.

AUTO-VALIDATION, run for real: bash packages/flutter_pear_example/tool/ios_hot_restart_gate.sh exited 0 end to end. Real log evidence: first boot -- flutter_pear example: reattached=false; after SIGUSR2 -- flutter_pear example: reattached=true; zero worklet_start_failed/BUNDLE_VERSION_MISMATCH lines; after background+relaunch, SIGUSR1 produced Reloaded 0 libraries in 83ms, proving the app process still responds. No Swift-host gap was exposed by the gate (DO step 4) -- the reattach/generation machinery from flutter_pear-ovt.3.1 worked correctly on every run, so nothing needed mirroring from the Kotlin comments this time.

Required a temporary local path-based BareKit.xcframework override for every validation run (same as flutter_pear-ovt.3.1) since the real repackedUrl in barekit-pin.json is still PENDING-UPLOAD per the accepted flutter_pear-ovt.2.3 decision -- reverted to the committed url:/checksum: state after every run, confirmed clean via git status.

MANUAL (DO step 5), checked once with real evidence: ran a real dart run flutter_pear_example:peer --topic ... chat peer against the app, confirmed baseline (no-restart) message delivery renders correctly on screen (screenshot evidence), then hot-restarted mid a 10-message burst from the peer. Result: NO CRASH (this task's own core concern) -- the app cleanly reattached (reattached=true) and re-showed Connected. However, no message sent by the peer after the restart rendered on screen, across two separate runs, even well after everything settled and including one final message sent long after the burst. Root-caused by reading the code (not guessed): packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/swarm.dart's PearSwarm.join() attaches its _rpc.events listener (which turns a replayed EventName.swarmConnection into a new PearConnection on the .connections broadcast stream) before the swarmJoin RPC round trip resolves, but every caller (main.dart's _join included) only calls .connections.listen() AFTER join() returns -- fine for a fresh join (the real peer connection takes real network time), but pear-end's Method.SWARM_JOIN handler replays swarmConnection SYNCHRONOUSLY for an already-connected peer on rejoin, which can be broadcast to zero listeners and permanently lost, a classic late-broadcast-subscriber race, the same class PrejoinedSwarmWiring already exists to solve for the QR-pairing flow but not for this plain-join path. Confirmed this is NOT Swift-host-specific (pear-end/index.js and swarm.dart are unmodified, shared Dart/JS code across both platforms) -- filed as a separate, clearly-scoped follow-up bug: flutter_pear-c2b (P1, since it means any hot restart while already connected can silently go message-deaf on either platform). Not fixed here per this task's own DO step 4 scoping (Swift-host gaps only) and closing instructions (file new work separately rather than expanding this issue).

melos run analyze: clean, 0 issues, all 4 packages. flutter_pear_example unit tests: 43 passed. flutter_pear/check_pins_test.dart re-verified clean (unaffected by this task).
close_reason: Scripted iOS hot-restart gate implemented and passing end-to-end for real (tool/ios_hot_restart_gate.sh): reattach with no reinstall, no worklet_start_failed, handshake passes before and after restart, app still responds after a background/relaunch cycle. Manual message-burst check done; found and filed a real, non-Swift-specific connection-replay race (flutter_pear-c2b) rather than expanding this task's scope. See --append-notes for full evidence.

title: Swift worklet host: boot, binary IPC, suspend/resume/terminate, crash backstop, reattach machinery
description:
CONTEXT: flutter_pear v0.2 adds iOS to flutter_pear_bare (worklet lifecycle + raw binary IPC only — keep it SMALL). The platform-neutral Dart side, packages/flutter_pear_bare/lib/src/bare_worklet.dart, defines the channel contract; the behavioral spec is the Kotlin host packages/flutter_pear_bare/android/src/main/kotlin/tech/loable/flutter_pear_bare/FlutterPearBarePlugin.kt (~326 heavily commented lines). Change NEITHER file's semantics. packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/ does not exist (TO BE CREATED). Contract to mirror EXACTLY: MethodChannel flutter_pear_bare/control — methods start (args map with bundlePath, returns map with reattached bool + generationId int), suspend, resume, terminate; error codes worklet_start_failed and worklet_not_started; native-to-Dart call onWorkletExit with reason + generationId. BasicMessageChannel flutter_pear_bare/ipc with FlutterStandardMessageCodec carrying raw byte arrays — MANDATORY, the Dart side is shared and Android deliberately rejected BinaryCodec (flutter/flutter#19849 class bug, see comments in both files). Frame length-prefixing happens in Dart; native relays raw bytes unmodified. Worklet + IPC live in Swift STATIC (type-level) state, not the plugin instance: a Dart hot restart recreates the plugin without killing the process, so start must detect the running worklet, re-arm the read loop, and return reattached=true with the UNCHANGED generationId; only a fresh boot bumps the generation (flutter_pear-3vh comments explain the stale-exit race). Crash backstop: the IPC read loop ending unexpectedly is the only native exit signal (bare-kit has no exit callback); report via onWorkletExit stamped with the generation captured when that loop was armed. References: react-native-bare-kit ios/ (same authors) and holepunchto/bare-kit v2.3.0 (Worklet + IPC types; Android pins bareKitVersion 2.3.0 in packages/flutter_pear_bare/android/build.gradle line 45). Bundle asset: Kotlin loads Flutter asset subpath assets/pear-end.bundle from package flutter_pear; on iOS use FlutterDartProject lookupKey(forAsset:fromPackage:) — first grep packages/flutter_pear/bin/pack.dart for bundleAssetPath in case the pack epic renamed or split it. argv[0] must be a writable per-app dir (packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/index.js line ~255 refuses to boot without it); pass the Application Support base — a sibling task hardens storage. Cross-epic prerequisites (verify first; if absent, stop and flag with bd human): committed packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/addons/*.xcframework and barekit-pin.json from the pack epic; spike epic T0/T1 passed.
DO:
1. Create the Flutter SPM plugin layout packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/flutter_pear_bare/Package.swift + Sources/flutter_pear_bare/ per docs.flutter.dev swift-package-manager/for-plugin-authors. Hand-seed Package.swift: library product, path binaryTargets for each committed ios/addons/*.xcframework, binaryTarget(name:url:checksum:) for BareKit with values copied from barekit-pin.json. (A later task makes :pack generate this file.)
2. Write Sources/flutter_pear_bare/FlutterPearBarePlugin.swift mirroring Kotlin one-for-one: static worklet/workletIpc/workletGeneration; startWorklet reattach branch; read-loop relay re-armed via DispatchQueue.main.async (mirrors Kotlin Handler.post — prevents unbounded recursion on synchronous delivery); stale-callback guard comparing the captured IPC identity; reportUnexpectedExit(reason, capturedGeneration) that nulls the statics then invokes onWorkletExit; terminate that nulls statics BEFORE closing IPC and terminating the worklet (freed-memory ordering, see Kotlin terminateWorklet); suspend/resume passthrough; attached flag cleared in detachFromEngine(for:).
3. All channel sends hop to the main thread (BareKit IPC callbacks can arrive off-main — Eng2 review made the codec + main-thread marshaling contract explicit).
4. Add ios: pluginClass: FlutterPearBarePlugin under plugin: platforms: in packages/flutter_pear_bare/pubspec.yaml (replacing the ponytail iOS-is-M1 comment near line 35).
5. If packages/flutter_pear_example/ios is absent, hydrate it: cd packages/flutter_pear_example and run flutter create --platforms=ios . — then ensure NSLocalNetworkUsageDescription exists in ios/Runner/Info.plist (copy the spike epic's entry from its bd notes).
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Channel names, method names, argument keys, result keys, and error codes match the Kotlin host exactly; bare_worklet.dart unmodified.
- start returns reattached=false + new generationId on fresh boot; reattached=true + unchanged generationId across a hot restart.
- Session nonce + version handshake passes unmodified: Pear.start succeeds against the Swift host with kPearEndBundleVersion (packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/bundle_version.dart) reported via attach.info.
- IPC bytes flow both directions unmodified; all channel sends on the main thread.
- onWorkletExit code path present, stamped with the captured generation.
AUTO-VALIDATION:
- xcrun simctl list devices available, then boot an iPhone sim with xcrun simctl boot if none is booted.
- cd packages/flutter_pear_example and run: flutter build ios --simulator --debug — must succeed with no manual Xcode edits beyond DO step 5.
- cd packages/flutter_pear_example and run: flutter test integration_test/ipc_transport_test.dart -d SIMID — must pass (exercises Pear.start handshake + IPC echo against the real worklet).
REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).
3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.
CONSTRAINTS: all P2P logic stays in JavaScript inside the Bare worklet and Dart is a typed remote control (never reimplement protocols in Dart); committed artifacts (bundle, jniLibs, xcframeworks) are versioned like source and a fresh checkout must never need :pack to build; new dependencies must be MIT/Apache-2.0/BSD/ISC; do not relitigate standing v0.2 decisions (single flutter_pear_bare package, committed addons, arm64-only sim slices).
notes:
IMPLEMENTED (Swift worklet host). Files: packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/flutter_pear_bare/Package.swift (new SPM plugin package: library product flutter-pear-bare hyphenated per Flutter own convention, targets flutter_pear_bare + CBareKit + BareKit binaryTarget url/checksum from barekit-pin.json + 12 addon binaryTargets), Sources/CBareKit/ (headers-only shim re-exporting BareKit.h as a Clang module since BareKit.xcframework ships no module map -- lets Swift import CBareKit instead of a bridging header, which SPM targets cannot use), Sources/flutter_pear_bare/FlutterPearBarePlugin.swift (mirrors FlutterPearBarePlugin.kt one for one: static worklet/workletIpc/workletGeneration, reattach branch, DispatchQueue.main.async re-arm + onWorkletExit marshaling, reportUnexpectedExit generation stamping, terminate ordering, detachFromEngine clearing attached). pubspec.yaml gained ios: pluginClass: FlutterPearBarePlugin.

Removed the throwaway T0/T1 spike (SpikeBareHost.swift, its BareKitShim local SPM package, and all pbxproj wiring) from flutter_pear_example/ios -- it registered the exact same flutter_pear_bare/control and flutter_pear_bare/ipc channel names as the real plugin and would have silently stolen the handler once both were present. AppDelegate.swift and the bridging header cleaned of spike references.

Real constraint discovered during validation, not previously documented anywhere: Flutter symlinks each SPM-plugin package directory into its own ephemeral FlutterGeneratedPluginSwiftPackage tree, and a ../-relative binaryTarget path inside that package resolves lexically against the outer symlink instead of following it first, landing outside the repo (confirmed via a real flutter build ios failure, does not contain a binary artifact). Fixed by committing a nested symlink packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/flutter_pear_bare/addons pointing at ../addons, so every addon path stays inside the plugin package own directory tree.

AUTO-VALIDATION, run for real: xcrun simctl list devices available (iPhone 17 Pro iOS 26.5 already booted, used directly). flutter build ios --simulator --debug succeeded (required a temporary local path-based BareKit override for the still-PENDING-UPLOAD real url per the accepted 2.3 decision -- reverted after every validation run, confirmed clean via git status). flutter test integration_test/ipc_transport_test.dart -d <sim> PASSED for real: Pear.start completed a genuine attach.info round trip against the real Swift host and real worklet boot. Mutation spot-check: disabled the native to Dart IPC forward in relayFromWorklet, rebuilt, confirmed the integration test fails with TimeoutException after 8s; reverted, confirmed passing again.

melos run analyze: clean, 0 issues, all 4 packages. Full suites: flutter_pear_bare 17 passed, flutter_pear 248 passed, flutter_pear_test 13 passed, flutter_pear_example unit tests 43 passed.

Bug fix along the way: check_pins.dart Swift-host asset-name detection assumed an inline lookupKey(forAsset: "literal") call site (matching the since-removed spike host's style) -- the real host factors the path into a named bundleAssetSubpath constant instead (matching the Kotlin host's own BUNDLE_ASSET_SUBPATH constant pattern), so the old regex never matched and silently kept reporting the Swift host as not landed. Fixed the regex to match the constant declaration; updated check_pins_test.dart's _swiftHost fixture helper to match. The exactly-3-skips real-repo test net stayed at 3 (two Package.swift PENDING-UPLOAD entries now instead of one, minus the Swift-host skip which now correctly clears) with zero mismatches.

Filed flutter_pear-2nd as a separate follow-up: repackBareKit's rezip step is not byte-deterministic (two --no-upload runs against identical upstream bytes produced two different repackedSha256 values) -- out of scope here, not a regression from this task.

NSLocalNetworkUsageDescription (DO step 5): flutter_pear_example/ios already existed (from the spike epic) so the if-absent hydration branch does not apply; confirmed via bd search that the Local Network Info.plist key is explicitly flutter_pear-ovt.4.1's scope, not this task's.

Full hot-restart reattach end-to-end verification (reattached=true, unchanged generationId across a real Dart hot restart) is flutter_pear-ovt.3.2's explicit scope per the epic breakdown -- the reattach branch itself is implemented and code-reviewed against the Kotlin host line by line, and covered by the same mutation-tested read-loop machinery, but a live hot-restart cannot be driven from a single flutter test integration_test run.
close_reason: Real Swift worklet host implemented and validated end-to-end: flutter build ios and a real integration_test attach.info round trip both pass against the real BareKit worklet on an iOS simulator, with a mutation spot-check confirming the IPC relay is actually exercised. Throwaway T0/T1 spike wiring removed to avoid a channel-name collision with the real plugin. See --append-notes for full evidence.

title: pear-end: reject symlink and path-escape entries in DRIVE_MIRROR_TO_DISK, emit drive.mirrorWarning (zip-slip fix)
description:
CONTEXT: verified zip-slip vulnerability (Eng2 finding 6; plan decision 36 — this is the ONLY pear-end change in v0.2). The DRIVE_MIRROR_TO_DISK handler (packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/index.js, case at line 980) mirrors an untrusted peer Hyperdrive to disk: it constructs a Localdrive on p.localDir and runs drive.mirror(localDrive) via mirror-drive. mirror-drive propagates remote symlink entries — packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/node_modules/mirror-drive/index.js around line 323 calls dst.symlink(key, srcEntry.value.linkname) whenever an entry has a linkname — and localdrive writes relative link targets uncontained. Attack: a malicious peer publishes symlink /x with target ../../.. then file /x/payload — arbitrary file write outside the mirror directory in the app sandbox. Fix decided in the plan: reject/skip symlink entries when mirroring untrusted drives (filter before mirror or wrap Localdrive) and reject containment-escaping paths; each rejection emits a typed warning event (DX2 finding 54 — a dev debugging a file that never arrived must see why). Event pattern: send an object with ev and p fields — see index.js line 674 (CORE_UPDATE); EventName lives in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/schema.js (map at line 80). A follow-up task surfaces the event in Dart; this task defines the wire contract exactly.

DO:
1. In pear-end/schema.js add DRIVE_MIRROR_WARNING with wire value drive.mirrorWarning to EventName (exact string — the Dart task pins it in schema.dart).
2. In the DRIVE_MIRROR_TO_DISK case, wrap the Localdrive destination: delegate everything to the real Localdrive EXCEPT (a) symlink(key, linkname) never writes — record a rejection with reason symlink-rejected; (b) every write-type operation mirror-drive invokes (createWriteStream, plus del/unlink if pruning applies) first resolves key against p.localDir using bare-path and rejects with reason path-escape when the resolved path is not strictly inside it.
3. Per rejection, send the event with ev = EventName.DRIVE_MIRROR_WARNING and p = { drive: p.drive, path: the entry key, reason } and increment a counter.
4. Add rejected: count to the DRIVE_MIRROR_TO_DISK response next to added/changed/removed (additive; the Dart typed result grows in the follow-up task).
5. New test packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/test/hostile-mirror.test.js (TO BE CREATED; run under node --test from pear-end/ — see index.test.js's header for the stubbed Bare/BareKit/IPC harness pattern and the bare-fs/bare-path require-cache seeding it needs under plain Node): build a real Hyperdrive in a temp corestore; publish a legit file, a symlink entry with an escaping target, and a file whose path routes through it; mirror to a temp dir via the real handler; assert the legit file arrived, nothing exists outside the mirror dir (probe the would-be escape path), warning events with correct drive/path/reason were captured on the stubbed send, and the response rejected count matches.
6. pear-end changed, so regenerate the committed bundle (dart run flutter_pear:pack from packages/flutter_pear — computeBundleVersion auto-bumps the baked version) and leave assets/pear-end.bundle in the working tree.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Symlink entries never reach disk during DRIVE_MIRROR_TO_DISK; escaping paths never write outside p.localDir.
- Every rejection emits drive.mirrorWarning with drive, path, reason; reasons are exactly symlink-rejected and path-escape.
- Response includes a rejected count; added/changed/removed semantics unchanged for benign drives.
- hostile-mirror.test.js covers the symlink entry, the escape probe, event capture, and the count — and passes.
- Full pear-end suite green; committed bundle regenerated.

AUTO-VALIDATION:
- cd packages/flutter_pear/pear-end && node --test test/hostile-mirror.test.js — all pass.
- cd packages/flutter_pear/pear-end && npm test — full suite green.
- Mutation spot-check: restore direct delegation of symlink to the real Localdrive (undo the rejection); node --test test/hostile-mirror.test.js must fail; revert.

REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).
3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.

CONSTRAINTS: all P2P logic stays in JavaScript inside the Bare worklet and Dart is a typed remote control (never reimplement protocols in Dart); committed artifacts (the regenerated bundle) are versioned like source; new dependencies must be MIT/Apache-2.0/BSD/ISC — prefer zero new deps here (wrap the existing Localdrive, do not add packages).
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION: added EventName.DRIVE_MIRROR_WARNING (wire value drive.mirrorWarning) to pear-end/schema.js. In index.js's DRIVE_MIRROR_TO_DISK handler, wrapped the Localdrive destination via a new guardMirrorDestination(localDrive, root, onReject) helper -- a Proxy (not a hand-rolled object) delegating everything to the real Localdrive except symlink (rejected UNCONDITIONALLY, never delegated -- symlinks are never legitimately needed for this drive-to-disk use case, and Localdrive.symlink() writes a relative linkname's target uncontained, only resolving an absolute-looking one against the drive root), createWriteStream and del (both resolve the key against root via a new isContainedPath helper -- path.relative-based, correctly rejecting a same-prefix sibling like /root-evil, not just naive startsWith -- and reject with path-escape if not strictly contained; createWriteStream returns a real discard Writable from streamx so mirror-drive's pipeline still completes cleanly instead of throwing). Chose a Proxy over a bespoke stand-in object after confirming mirror-drive also calls .ready()/.entry()/.core/.getBlobs on the destination (verified by reading mirror-drive's own source) -- a hand-rolled object would need to track every one of those exactly and silently break on a future mirror-drive addition. Every rejection calls onReject(key, reason), which sends { ev: EventName.DRIVE_MIRROR_WARNING, p: { drive: p.drive, path: key, reason } } and increments a counter; the response now includes rejected alongside added/changed/removed.

TEST: created pear-end/test/hostile-mirror.test.js. Builds a REAL Hyperdrive (not a mock) in a temp corestore seeded at the exact path index.js derives from Bare.argv[0] (<argv[0]>/pear-corestore) -- publishes /legit.txt, a symlink /x -> a relative path escaping well past the mirror directory, and /x/payload (the entry that would route through the symlink); closes that seed store, then boots the worklet against the SAME storage directory and calls the REAL DRIVE_MIRROR_TO_DISK RPC handler end to end via the exact IPC wire framing (no fake replication needed, since the worklet's own Corestore finds the pre-seeded local data directly). A custom bootWorklet (self-contained, not imported from index.test.js) also tracks and destroys the Hyperswarm instance index.js constructs unconditionally at module load (same fix index.test.js's own header documents -- an undestroyed one hangs node --test forever) and captures raw {ev,p} event frames alongside the usual request/response ones. Asserts: legit.txt arrived; destDir/x exists only as a plain mkdir'd directory (never an actual symlink, confirmed via fs.lstatSync(...).isSymbolicLink()); the actual escape target (the symlink's relative destination) was never written to -- the real zip-slip probe; exactly one drive.mirrorWarning event with the correct drive/path/reason; response.rejected === 1; and /x/payload still mirrors normally as an ordinary nested file (rejecting the symlink doesn't reject its former key-namespace siblings, since with the symlink gone there's nothing left to escape through).

AUTO-VALIDATION EVIDENCE:
- node --test test/hostile-mirror.test.js: 1/1 pass (after fixing two authoring mistakes found along the way: forgot the bare-fs/bare-path require-cache stub index.js needs under plain Node, and a wrong assertion in my own test expecting destDir/x to not exist at all rather than just not be a symlink).
- npm test (full pear-end suite, the real gate -- --test-concurrency=1): 35/35 pass. (A bare ✔ lww: two writers racing the same key converge to the identical winner on both sides (386.232541ms)
✔ lww: the SAME concurrent race, with the two appends made in the opposite code order, still converges to one agreed winner (arrival order shouldn't matter) (169.082958ms)
✔ lww: apply() never crashes/closes the base on a malformed op from a peer -- it is skipped, and later valid ops from the SAME batch still apply (121.681167ms)

    operator: 'deepStrictEqual',
    diff: 'simple'
  } without that flag showed one unrelated autobase-recipes.test.js CRDT test flake under concurrent load -- confirmed pre-existing and unrelated by re-running it both in isolation and via the real npm test script, both green; nothing in this task touches autobase-recipes.js.)
- Mutation spot-check: restored direct delegation of symlink to the real Localdrive (undo the rejection); node --test test/hostile-mirror.test.js failed as expected (the un-rejected symlink got created for real, then the subsequent /x/payload write threw ENOENT trying to follow it to a nonexistent target -- exactly the kind of failure this fix prevents). Reverted; confirmed clean.
- dart run flutter_pear:pack (packages/flutter_pear): regenerated the committed bundle (pear-end changed, computeBundleVersion auto-bumped), jniLibs, and ios/addons -- exit 0.
- melos run analyze (repo root): No issues found across all 3 packages.
- flutter test (full suite, packages/flutter_pear): all 226 tests pass, unaffected (this task only touches pear-end JS).
- test/pack_android_regression_test.dart: still passes.
- No git commit/push performed (manual-commit-only rule).
close_reason: Zip-slip fix landed: guardMirrorDestination (a Proxy over the real Localdrive) unconditionally rejects symlink entries and rejects containment-escaping createWriteStream/del keys in DRIVE_MIRROR_TO_DISK, emitting drive.mirrorWarning per rejection and a rejected count in the response. hostile-mirror.test.js proves it end to end against a real Hyperdrive with a real malicious symlink+escape-routed file, via the real RPC handler. Full pear-end suite 35/35, mutation spot-check confirmed, bundle regenerated, analyze clean, flutter_pear suite 226/226 unaffected.

title: pack: BareKit repack + rehost step with barekit-pin.json two-link checksum chain
description:
CONTEXT: Eng2 finding 1, CRITICAL. Android ships BareKit via a checksum-pinned Gradle fetch at consumer build time: packages/flutter_pear_bare/android/build.gradle defines bareKitVersion = 2.3.0 (line 45) with a pinned SHA256 and downloads https://github.com/holepunchto/bare-kit/releases/download/v2.3.0/prebuilds.zip (line ~124). iOS consumers will get BareKit via an SPM binaryTarget with url and checksum — but upstream holepunchto/bare-kit releases ship ONE multi-platform prebuilds.zip (~354MB), and SPM URL binary targets require a zip with the xcframework at archive root; pointing SPM at upstream would both fail layout and pull 354MB per consumer. Decision (plan decision 31): :pack gains a maintainer repack step — download the pinned upstream zip, verify its SHA256, extract BareKit.xcframework (the iOS slices), re-zip at archive root, upload as a release asset on THIS repo (gh release upload), and write the committed pin manifest packages/flutter_pear_bare/barekit-pin.json (TO BE CREATED) with the two-link checksum chain: bareKitVersion, upstreamUrl, upstreamSha256, repackedUrl, repackedSha256. Provenance becomes maintainer-repacked with an auditable chain (recorded in the plan's threat model). DX2 finding 50: bareKitVersion must come from the Gradle file (single source) so Android and iOS provably pin the same BareKit release — pack.dart already regex-reads it in _addBareKitStaticEntry (lines 526-552).

DO:
1. Add repackBareKit(pkgRoot) to pack.dart, wired into main(). Idempotence: on a routine run, short-circuit with no download when barekit-pin.json exists, its bareKitVersion matches the Gradle value, and repackedUrl answers an HTTP HEAD; a --repack-barekit flag forces the full pipeline. Document this in the file header.
2. Parse bareKitVersion and the expected upstream SHA256 out of packages/flutter_pear_bare/android/build.gradle (extend the existing regex approach); fail loud if either is missing.
3. Download the upstream zip to a temp dir, compute SHA256 (package:crypto is already imported in pack.dart), compare to the Gradle pin — mismatch is a hard nonzero failure before any upload.
4. Extract only BareKit.xcframework and re-zip with the xcframework directory at archive root; compute repackedSha256 over the new zip (swift package compute-checksum is SHA256 of the zip, so values match).
5. Upload via gh: create release tag barekit-v<version> if absent (gh release create), then gh release upload with asset name BareKit-<version>-ios.xcframework.zip; derive owner/repo from gh repo view --json nameWithOwner; repackedUrl is the resulting releases/download URL.
6. Write packages/flutter_pear_bare/barekit-pin.json (pretty-printed JSON, the five fields plus a generatedBy note) — a committed artifact.
7. Tests in packages/flutter_pear/test/pack_ios_test.dart (create the file if the addons task has not yet): pin-file shape (five keys, 64-char lowercase hex checksums, https URLs, version matching a fixture gradle file); upstream-checksum mismatch on a fixture zip fails loud; missing bareKitVersion fails loud. Keep network and gh out of unit tests — factor download/upload behind injectable functions and unit-test the pure parts.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- dart run flutter_pear:pack --repack-barekit performs download, verify, repack, upload, and writes barekit-pin.json; a following plain run short-circuits without downloading.
- barekit-pin.json contains bareKitVersion, upstreamUrl, upstreamSha256, repackedUrl, repackedSha256 (plus generatedBy), and bareKitVersion equals the Gradle pin.
- Downloading repackedUrl yields a zip whose SHA256 equals repackedSha256 and whose root entry is BareKit.xcframework/ — the chain verifies end-to-end.
- Upstream checksum mismatch aborts nonzero before any upload.
- New tests pass without network access.

AUTO-VALIDATION:
- cd packages/flutter_pear && dart run flutter_pear:pack --repack-barekit — exits 0, writes ../flutter_pear_bare/barekit-pin.json (needs gh auth and a ~354MB download; use the session scratchpad for temp space).
- Read repackedUrl and repackedSha256 from the pin with python3 json; curl -sL the URL into the scratchpad; shasum -a 256 must equal repackedSha256; unzip -l must show BareKit.xcframework/ as the root entry.
- python3 one-liner asserting the pin's bareKitVersion equals the bareKitVersion string in packages/flutter_pear_bare/android/build.gradle.
- cd packages/flutter_pear && flutter test test/pack_ios_test.dart — pass.
- Mutation spot-check: make the code skip the upstream sha compare; the checksum-mismatch fixture test must fail; revert.

REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).
3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.

CONSTRAINTS: committed artifacts are versioned like source (the pin manifest is committed; BareKit binaries themselves stay OUT of git and the pub archive); new dependencies must be MIT/Apache-2.0/BSD/ISC (BareKit is Apache-2.0); do not relitigate the standing v0.2 decisions; every public symbol documented; this repo has no CI — everything runs by hand.
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION: added readBareKitGradlePin(pkgRoot) (parses bareKitVersion + bareKitSha256 from build.gradle, throws BareKitPinException if either missing) and repackBareKit(pkgRoot, {force, upload, downloadFn, uploadFn}) to bin/pack.dart, wired into main() (runs every pack invocation; --repack-barekit forces a full re-run past the idempotence check; --no-upload skips the gh publish step). Idempotence: short-circuits with no download when barekit-pin.json already records the current bareKitVersion and its repackedUrl still resolves via a live HEAD request. Pipeline: download the pinned upstream prebuilds.zip, verify its SHA256 against build.gradle's pin (hard abort before any upload on mismatch), extract just apple/BareKit.xcframework (per flutter_pear-ovt.1.2's spike finding on the in-zip path), re-zip it at archive root, compute repackedSha256, then either really publish via gh release create+upload (default) or write an explicit PENDING-UPLOAD sentinel string in place of repackedUrl when uploading is disabled. download/upload are injectable (downloadFn/uploadFn) so unit tests never touch the network or gh, per the task's own DO step 7.

DEVELOPER DECISION THIS SESSION: asked via AskUserQuestion whether to run the actual gh release upload for real; developer chose do the repack, skip the upload. Per that explicit choice, I ran the REAL end-to-end pipeline (dart run flutter_pear:pack --repack-barekit --no-upload, TMPDIR pointed at the session scratchpad) -- a genuine ~354MB download of the real upstream v2.3.0 prebuilds.zip, real SHA256 verification against build.gradle's pin (matched), real extraction/re-zip, real repackedSha256 computation -- but deliberately did NOT run the actual gh release create/upload commands. packages/flutter_pear_bare/barekit-pin.json is committed to the working tree with bareKitVersion=2.3.0 and upstreamSha256 matching build.gradle exactly, repackedSha256 a real computed checksum, and repackedUrl left as the PENDING-UPLOAD sentinel string repackBareKit writes for this exact case (documented in the function's own doc comment). This is an intentional, explicitly pre-approved gap, not a defect -- finishing it for real (running with uploading enabled) is a manual follow-up whenever the developer wants to actually publish the release asset.

REAL BUG FOUND AND FIXED ALONG THE WAY: bin/check_pins.dart (built earlier this session, task 5.3, before barekit-pin.json existed) had guessed at simple top-level version/sha256 keys for barekit-pin.json's schema. Task 2.3's own DO/ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA text (repeated verbatim twice) specifies five fields named bareKitVersion, upstreamUrl, upstreamSha256, repackedUrl, repackedSha256 plus generatedBy -- a genuine, real naming mismatch between the two pieces once the real producer landed, caught immediately because writing the REAL pin file made check_pins_test.dart fail for real (not a fixture assumption -- an actual contract mismatch). Fixed check_pins.dart to read bareKitVersion/upstreamSha256 (matching repackBareKit's real, spec-mandated output) instead of version/sha256; updated check_pins_test.dart's fixtures accordingly (4 call sites) and its real-repo-state test's expected skip count from 4 to 3 (barekit-pin.json genuinely no longer skipped now that it exists for real).

Created test/pack_ios_test.dart's new BareKit-repack group (5 tests, network/gh-free via injected downloadFn/uploadFn): readBareKitGradlePin parses correctly / throws on missing bareKitVersion / throws on missing bareKitSha256; repackBareKit(upload:false) writes a well-shaped pin (five keys, both checksums real 64-hex-char lowercase, upstreamUrl is https, repackedUrl is the PENDING-UPLOAD sentinel) and never calls uploadFn; repackBareKit aborts nonzero on an upstream checksum mismatch, before ever calling uploadFn, and never writes a pin file in that case.

AUTO-VALIDATION EVIDENCE:
- dart run flutter_pear:pack --repack-barekit --no-upload: exit 0, wrote packages/flutter_pear_bare/barekit-pin.json for real (see DEVELOPER DECISION above for why --no-upload was used).
- python3 json comparison: pin bareKitVersion (2.3.0) equals build.gradle's bareKitVersion (2.3.0) -- match confirmed.
- dart run flutter_pear:check_pins (no --strict): All pins agree (7 checked, 3 leg(s) skipped as not-yet-landed) -- down from 4 skips before this task, confirming barekit-pin.json is now genuinely recognized and cross-checked with zero mismatches. dart run flutter_pear:check_pins --strict: still exits 1 (3 pins out of sync: Package.swift/podspec/Swift-host, all genuinely not landed yet -- epics 2.4/3.6/3.1), down from 4 before this task.
- flutter test test/pack_ios_test.dart: all 16 tests pass (11 from flutter_pear-ovt.2.2 plus 5 new).
- Mutation spot-check: made the upstream checksum compare unconditionally skip (if (false && ...)); the checksum-mismatch fixture test failed as expected (pipeline proceeded all the way to the injected uploadFn instead of aborting). Reverted; confirmed clean.
- flutter test test/check_pins_test.dart: all 10 tests pass (after the bareKitVersion/upstreamSha256 field-name fix described above).
- melos run analyze (repo root): No issues found across all 3 packages.
- flutter test (full suite, packages/flutter_pear): all 231 tests pass.
- Disk space monitored throughout the real 354MB download+extraction: stayed healthy (16-20GB free), no leftover temp files (pack.dart's finally-block cleanup confirmed working).
- No git commit/push performed (manual-commit-only rule). No gh release create/upload commands were run, per the developer's explicit choice this session.
close_reason: repackBareKit + readBareKitGradlePin added to pack.dart (injectable download/upload, idempotent, --repack-barekit/--no-upload flags). Ran the REAL pipeline (354MB download, real checksum verify, real extraction/re-zip) per the developer's explicit choice to skip the actual gh publish step -- barekit-pin.json committed with real bareKitVersion/upstreamSha256/repackedSha256 and a documented PENDING-UPLOAD sentinel for repackedUrl, a pre-approved intentional gap. Found and fixed a real schema-naming bug in check_pins.dart (guessed version/sha256 keys vs the spec-mandated bareKitVersion/upstreamSha256) discovered because the real pin file now exists. check_pins now shows 3 skips/0 mismatches (down from 4). pack_ios_test.dart 16/16, check_pins_test.dart 10/10, mutation spot-check confirmed, analyze clean, full suite 231/231.

title: pack: link iOS addon xcframeworks into committed flutter_pear_bare/ios/addons (device + sim arm64 only)
description:
CONTEXT: linkNativeAddons (packages/flutter_pear/bin/pack.dart lines 103-176) is the Android precedent: it runs bare-link with --host android-arm64 --host android-x64 from packages/flutter_pear/pear-end (requires pear-end/node_modules — run npm install there first) into a temp dir, verifies EVERY ABI produced at least one .so BEFORE touching the committed tree, then wipes and rewrites packages/flutter_pear_bare/android/src/main/jniLibs/<abi>/ and prunes stale ABI dirs. Decision flutter_pear-k2y: native addons are committed maintainer-time artifacts, never consumer-build-time resolution (external consumers otherwise shipped APKs missing every addon and crashed on Pear.start with ADDON_NOT_FOUND). The v0.2 plan applies the same decision to iOS: committed packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/addons/*.xcframework (TO BE CREATED — packages/flutter_pear_bare has NO ios/ directory today), with device arm64 (ios-arm64) plus simulator arm64 (ios-arm64-simulator) slices ONLY (decision D21; no x86_64 sim slice — Intel Macs get docs in a later task). Reference implementation: react-native-bare-kit (same authors as holepunchto/bare-kit) vendors ios/BareKit.xcframework plus ios/addons/*.xcframework assembled by its ios/link.mjs script — the blueprint for the fallback. Primary mechanism: bare-link with --host ios-arm64 --host ios-arm64-simulator (the spike epic verified bare-link's iOS support surface — check its bd notes). Fallback if bare-link cannot emit iOS output: harvest prebuilds/ios-* Mach-O dylibs from the addon npm packages under pear-end/node_modules and structure each addon into an xcframework yourself (xcodebuild -create-xcframework, or hand-built Info.plist layout the way link.mjs does), owning install names and slice layout.

DO:
1. Add linkNativeAddonsIos(pkgRoot) to pack.dart (or extend linkNativeAddons), called from main() after the Android link step.
2. Replicate the Android fail-loud contract: build into a temp dir; verify at least one xcframework was produced AND every xcframework's Info.plist lists both ios-arm64 and ios-arm64-simulator in AvailableLibraries; only then delete and rewrite packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/addons/, pruning entries for addons that no longer exist; on any shortfall return nonzero and leave the committed directory untouched.
3. Keep Android outputs byte-identical for unchanged inputs (the plan's CRITICAL regression rule protects jniLibs and the Android bundle).
4. Skip-with-a-note (return 0) when pear-end/node_modules is missing, matching linkNativeAddons' documented behavior.
5. Create packages/flutter_pear/test/pack_ios_test.dart (TO BE CREATED, plan 5A): committed-layout assertions (every entry under ios/addons is a directory named *.xcframework whose Info.plist names both required slices) plus a fixture test that an empty link result returns nonzero without touching a pre-populated destination (mirror the fixture style in test/pack_test.dart). Layout assertions read the committed tree — no toolchain, no self-skip.
6. Run the real pack once so the committed artifacts exist in the working tree.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/addons/ exists with one .xcframework per pear-end native addon (expect at least the sodium-native and udx-native families).
- Every committed xcframework contains exactly the ios-arm64 and ios-arm64-simulator slices; no x86_64 anywhere under ios/addons.
- A produced-nothing run exits nonzero and leaves previously committed files untouched.
- Android jniLibs unchanged given unchanged pear-end inputs.
- test/pack_ios_test.dart exists and passes.

AUTO-VALIDATION:
- cd packages/flutter_pear && dart run flutter_pear:pack — exits 0, prints the ios/addons write.
- ls packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/addons — lists .xcframework directories.
- grep -L ios-arm64-simulator packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/addons/*.xcframework/Info.plist — prints nothing (every plist names the sim slice).
- cd packages/flutter_pear && flutter test test/pack_ios_test.dart — all pass.
- Mutation spot-check: drop ios-arm64-simulator from the required-slices list in the verification code; the pack_ios_test fixture test for short-slice rejection must fail; revert.

REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).
3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.

CONSTRAINTS: committed artifacts are versioned like source and a fresh checkout must never need :pack to build; do not relitigate the standing v0.2 decisions (committed addons, arm64-only sim slices, single flutter_pear_bare package); new dependencies must be MIT/Apache-2.0/BSD/ISC (reject GPL/AGPL/LGPL/MPL/SSPL/unlicensed); every public symbol documented.
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION: added linkNativeAddonsIos(pkgRoot) to bin/pack.dart, called from main() right after the Android linkNativeAddons step. Uses bare-link --host ios-arm64 --host ios-arm64-simulator (iosAddonHosts const, verified real and native by flutter_pear-ovt.1.5 -- no manual harvesting/xcodebuild fallback needed) into a temp dir, verifies at least one .xcframework was produced AND every xcframework's Info.plist text-contains both required <string>ios-arm64</string> and <string>ios-arm64-simulator</string> LibraryIdentifier entries BEFORE touching the committed tree, then wipes/rewrites packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/addons/ (pruning entries for addons no longer produced), matching the Android linkNativeAddons fail-loud-before-wiping contract exactly. Added a small recursive _copyDirectorySync helper (dart:io has no built-in cp -R) since xcframeworks are nested bundles, unlike the flat .so files the Android path copies. Created packages/flutter_pear/test/pack_ios_test.dart with layout assertions (reads the committed tree, no toolchain/self-skip) plus a fixture test for the produced-nothing-leaves-destination-untouched case and a missing-node_modules no-op case.

AUTO-VALIDATION EVIDENCE:
