- ls packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/addons: 12 xcframeworks (bare-fs, bare-inspect, bare-os, bare-type, bare-url, fs-native-extensions, quickbit-native, rabin-native, rocksdb-native, simdle-native, sodium-native, udx-native) -- includes both explicitly-required families.
- grep -L ios-arm64-simulator packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/addons/*.xcframework/Info.plist: empty (every plist names the sim slice).
- grep for x86_64 across all 12 Info.plists: none found (D21 arm64-only sim slice honored).
- flutter test test/pack_ios_test.dart: all 6 tests pass.
- Mutation spot-check (real, not mocked): temporarily changed the bare-link invocation to request only --host ios-arm64 (dropping the simulator host) while leaving the iosAddonHosts-driven verification loop unchanged -- bare-link then produced plain (non-xcframework) single-host output, correctly triggering the 'produced no .xcframework bundles' rejection path: dart run flutter_pear:pack exited 1 (confirmed via proper exit-code capture, not a piped tail), and the previously-committed ios/addons/ (all 12, both slices intact) was left completely untouched. Reverted; re-ran the real pack to restore the correct committed state.
- test/pack_android_regression_test.dart: still passes -- Android bundle + jniLibs byte-identical, unaffected by the iOS addon work.
- melos run analyze (repo root): No issues found across all 3 packages.
- flutter test (full suite, packages/flutter_pear): all 226 tests pass (was 220 before this task; +6 new).
- Committed artifact size: packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/addons/ is 21MB total -- well under the later size-guard task's 95MB per-artifact ceiling.
- No git commit/push performed (manual-commit-only rule).
close_reason: linkNativeAddonsIos added to pack.dart, wired into main() after the Android link step; 12 addon xcframeworks (both ios-arm64 + ios-arm64-simulator slices, no x86_64) committed into packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/addons/. Fail-loud-before-wiping contract mirrors the Android path exactly. pack_ios_test.dart (6 tests) + full suite (226/226) pass, mutation spot-check confirmed real rejection + untouched-destination behavior, Android regression clean, analyze clean.

title: pack: emit a multi-host (Android+iOS) pear-end bundle, replacing --preset android
description:
CONTEXT: dart run flutter_pear:pack (packages/flutter_pear/bin/pack.dart) builds the committed worklet bundle assets/pear-end.bundle via bare-pack. buildBundle (pack.dart line 257) currently invokes bare-pack with --preset android (line 269), shorthand for --linked --host android-arm --host android-arm64 --host android-ia32 --host android-x64 — so the committed bundle cannot boot on iOS (verified plan catch, pack.dart:247 region). The v0.2 plan decision (Eng2 finding 3, auto-decided) is ONE multi-host bundle, because Flutter assets are platform-unconditional: per-platform bundles would ship both bundles (~2MB each) to both platforms and falsify the v0.2 changelog promise that no iOS payload enters an Android build. iOS slices are device arm64 plus simulator arm64 only (decision D21). The asset path constant bundleAssetPath (pack.dart line 20, value assets/pear-end.bundle) is the loader contract with flutter_pear_bare and is declared in packages/flutter_pear/pubspec.yaml (assets section near line 62) — the multi-host path keeps it unchanged. nativeAddonAbis (pack.dart line 56) documents the deliberate 32-bit exclusion; mirror it. The spike epic validated bare-pack multi-host feasibility — check bd (bd list, bd show on the spike epic tasks) for its recorded answer before taking the fallback branch. The bundle is a committed, versioned artifact: regenerate it and leave it in the working tree.

DO:
1. In buildBundle, replace the --preset android args with an explicit list: --linked --host android-arm64 --host android-x64 --host ios-arm64 --host ios-arm64-simulator. Extract the host list into a documented top-level const (e.g. bundleHosts) so tests can assert it without running bare-pack.
2. Update buildBundle's doc comment (it currently documents the preset).
3. From packages/flutter_pear (after npm install in pear-end/ and npm i -g bare-pack if missing) run dart run flutter_pear:pack and confirm bare-pack accepts the combined host list and writes one bundle.
4. Extend test/pack_test.dart: assert bundleHosts is exactly the four hosts above and no --preset arg remains. The existing bare-pack test self-skips when the tool is missing (comment near test/pack_test.dart line 34) — new assertions must not add silent skips and must not require bare-pack installed.
5. FALLBACK, only if bare-pack hard-rejects combined android+ios hosts: emit assets/pear-end.bundle (android hosts, unchanged name) plus assets/pear-end-ios.bundle; write both atomically (temp files, rename only after both succeed); assert both bundles bake the identical version string equal to kPearEndBundleVersion from lib/src/bundle_version.dart (writeBundleVersion, pack.dart line 302, embeds it); add the new asset to pubspec.yaml; extend pack_test.dart to pin the Android ASSET SET; measure the example-app APK delta (flutter build apk --debug in packages/flutter_pear_example, then unzip -l build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/app-debug.apk) and record it with bd update --append-notes; amend packages/flutter_pear/CHANGELOG.md so it no longer claims none of the iOS payload enters an Android build.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- buildBundle passes no --preset flag; hosts are exactly android-arm64, android-x64, ios-arm64, ios-arm64-simulator.
- dart run flutter_pear:pack exits 0 and rewrites assets/pear-end.bundle at the unchanged asset path.
- grep -ac ios-arm64 assets/pear-end.bundle finds at least one match (linked addon specifiers embed host names).
- test/pack_test.dart pins the host list and passes.
- If the fallback branch was taken: both bundles written atomically, baked versions asserted equal to kPearEndBundleVersion, pubspec asset added, Android asset set pinned in pack_test.dart, APK delta recorded in this issue, CHANGELOG wording amended.

AUTO-VALIDATION:
- cd packages/flutter_pear && dart run flutter_pear:pack — exits 0, prints wrote assets/pear-end.bundle (export PATH with $HOME/development/flutter/bin prepended if dart is not found).
- grep -ac ios-arm64 packages/flutter_pear/assets/pear-end.bundle — prints a number of at least 1.
- cd packages/flutter_pear && flutter test test/pack_test.dart — all pass.

notes:
NOTE ON PARENT GATING: epic 2 (this task's parent) has an explicit bd blocks dependency from epic 1 (the spike epic), which remains open (1.8 hardware-blocked/flagged human, 1.10 open, 1.12 TCC spike not yet attempted, 1.14 close-out audit pending). Proceeding into epic 2's technical work per explicit developer confirmation this session -- the actual feasibility prerequisites epic 2 depends on (bare-pack multi-host bundle feasibility, bare-link iOS addon feasibility, determinism) were already established and closed via flutter_pear-ovt.1.5/1.6, so the remaining epic-1 gaps (hardware-blocked T2 chat smoke, TCC spike, close-out audit) are not substantive blockers to this specific task's technical content.

IMPLEMENTATION: extracted a top-level bundleHosts const (bin/pack.dart) with exactly 4 hosts -- android-arm64, android-x64, ios-arm64, ios-arm64-simulator -- dropping the 32-bit android-arm/android-ia32 hosts the OLD inline list carried, matching nativeAddonAbis' already-2-ABI-only decision (which this task's own context flagged as inconsistent). buildBundle now builds its bare-pack --host args from bundleHosts via a for-in spread instead of an inline 6-item list. Updated buildBundle's doc comment accordingly. No --preset flag anywhere (already true before this task, from flutter_pear-ovt.1.6).

AUTO-VALIDATION EVIDENCE:
- dart run flutter_pear:pack: exit 0, wrote assets/pear-end.bundle + jniLibs + THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES + NOTICE.
- grep -ac ios-arm64 assets/pear-end.bundle: 0 matches, NOT the >=1 the acceptance criteria/auto-validation text expects. Investigated thoroughly: bare-pack's manifest format (inspected directly, addons array uses generic linked:libNAME.so / linked:NAME.framework/NAME entries with NO host-triple substrings at all) never embeds literal host strings like ios-arm64 or android-arm64 anywhere, regardless of which/how many --host flags are passed -- confirmed via bare-pack --help (no verbosity/host-echo flag exists) and via a direct isolated bare-pack invocation (silent stdout, same empty grep result). This was equally true of the OLD 6-host list before this task -- not a regression this task introduced, a pre-existing inaccuracy in the task's own auto-validation text, never empirically checked against real bare-pack output when written. Substituted evidence that the substantive intent (iOS hosts are genuinely built in) holds: (a) bare-pack accepted the 2-ios-host request and exited 0 with no rejection; (b) the regenerated bundle is BYTE-IDENTICAL (sha256 d7122e56...c2d4bcdc1) to the bundle this exact repo already had committed under the OLD 6-host list, proving the 32-bit hosts were always inert/no-ops in bare-pack's --linked mode and iOS-host inclusion is unaffected by this cleanup; (c) flutter_pear-ovt.1.7 (already closed) already proved a multi-host bundle built with this exact bare-pack invocation shape (including ios-arm64 + ios-arm64-simulator) boots on a real iOS simulator with a passing version handshake -- the strongest possible functional proof.
- flutter test test/pack_test.dart: all 17 tests pass, including the new bundleHosts pin test.
- Mutation spot-check: changed ios-arm64-simulator to ios-arm64-sim in bundleHosts, ran flutter test test/pack_test.dart --plain-name bundleHosts: FAILED as expected (Which: at location [3] is ios-arm64-sim instead of ios-arm64-simulator); reverted, confirmed clean.
- test/pack_android_regression_test.dart: still passes (bundle + jniLibs byte-identical to the newly-committed-in-working-tree state) -- no Android drift from this change.
- melos run analyze (repo root): No issues found across all 3 packages.
- flutter test (full suite, packages/flutter_pear): all 220 tests pass.
- No git commit/push performed (manual-commit-only rule).
close_reason: bundleHosts extracted to exactly 4 hosts (android-arm64, android-x64, ios-arm64, ios-arm64-simulator), matching nativeAddonAbis' 2-ABI decision; no --preset. Bundle regenerates byte-identical to the prior 6-host output (32-bit hosts were always inert in --linked mode). One auto-validation bullet (grep -ac ios-arm64) is unsatisfiable due to a pre-existing inaccuracy in bare-pack's assumed manifest format, documented with substituted evidence (byte-identical regen, exit 0, and flutter_pear-ovt.1.7's already-closed real simulator boot proof). 17/17 pack_test.dart pass, mutation spot-check confirmed, pack_android_regression_test.dart clean, analyze clean, full suite 220/220.

title: FEAS-5: CocoaPods external-consumer spike — prove a build-time-fetched framework links
description:
CONTEXT: Epic flutter_pear-ovt.1 feasibility check 5 (Eng2 #12) — this answer MUST exist before any demo-gate (T3) work in later epics. The planned compat podspec fetches BareKit.xcframework at BUILD time via a script_phase, but CocoaPods resolves vendored-framework references at pod install — open question: does a framework that only appears on disk at build time actually link? prepare_command is NOT an option: CocoaPods never runs prepare_command for :path-installed pods (CocoaPods issue #2187), and Flutter installs ALL plugin pods exactly that way (ios/.symlinks/plugins development pods). The consumer must be EXTERNAL — a throwaway app in the session scratchpad, outside the melos workspace; packages/flutter_pear_example does not qualify. Input artifact: .spike/BareKit.xcframework at the repo root (gitignored) stands in for the downloaded framework (a local copy simulates the fetch).
DO:
1. In the scratchpad, create a throwaway pod: a podspec declaring vendored_frameworks pointing at a BareKit.xcframework path that does NOT exist at pod install time, plus a script_phase (execution_position before_compile) that copies .spike/BareKit.xcframework into that path, printing a distinctive marker — simulating the build-time fetch.
2. Create a throwaway external consumer in the scratchpad: a plain Xcode iOS app with a Podfile referencing the pod via :path (or a fresh flutter create app there). Add Swift code that references a BareKit symbol (import BareKit and instantiate a worklet type) so linking is actually exercised.
3. Run pod install (must succeed with the framework absent), then xcodebuild build for the iOS simulator. Determine whether the script_phase ran before compile/link and whether the app linked BareKit; inspect the built product with otool -L.
4. If the base recipe fails, try the documented redirects in order and record which one works: (a) a different script_phase execution_position, (b) install-time generation via a Podfile post_install hook, (c) a real pod dependency carrying the framework. The deliverable is the ANSWER — which mechanism is viable.
5. bd update flutter_pear-ovt.1 --append-notes with a note starting FEAS-PODS-CONSUMER: giving YES/NO for the build-time-fetch recipe, the working variant if redirected, and command evidence.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- pod install succeeds while the vendored framework is absent from disk.
- Either the build links the build-time-fetched BareKit (YES), or the failure is captured and a working redirect variant identified (NO plus redirect).
- Evidence includes the xcodebuild outcome and otool -L output on the built binary.
- All experiment files live in the scratchpad; repo working tree untouched.
- The epic note begins FEAS-PODS-CONSUMER:.
AUTO-VALIDATION:
- xcodebuild build -sdk iphonesimulator on the consumer exits 0 for the working variant, and its log shows the script_phase marker before the compile/link phases.
- otool -L <built app binary> | grep -c BareKit prints at least 1 for the working variant.
- bd show flutter_pear-ovt.1 | grep -c FEAS-PODS-CONSUMER prints at least 1.
REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).
3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.
CONSTRAINTS: record-only feasibility (the real compat podspec is resequenced to a later epic, post-T3); do not relitigate script_phase-over-prepare_command or SPM-first decisions; new deps MIT/Apache-2.0/BSD/ISC only (none expected).
notes: AUTO-VALIDATION: for the WORKING variant (pre_install redirect): xcodebuild build -sdk iphonesimulator exits 0 (BUILD SUCCEEDED), and the log shows the pre_install marker printed during 'pod install' (before Xcode project generation even starts) -- earlier in the pipeline than the AUTO-VALIDATION text's own script_phase framing anticipated, but strictly stronger evidence of before-compile ordering. otool -L on the built Runner.debug.dylib | grep -c BareKit -> 1 (@rpath/BareKit.framework/BareKit). bd show flutter_pear-ovt.1 | grep -c FEAS-PODS-CONSUMER -> 1. Base recipe (script_phase only) was also fully exercised and demonstrated to fail once a real (non-canImport-guarded) symbol reference was forced -- recorded as the NO half of the answer. melos run analyze: clean, repo untouched (all spike files lived under the session scratchpad, confirmed via git status).
close_reason: FEAS-PODS-CONSUMER answered: base recipe (build-time script_phase fetch) does NOT work -- CocoaPods' FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS wiring is fixed at pod-install-time xcframework analysis and can't see a file that doesn't exist yet, confirmed by forcing a real (non-canImport-guarded) BareKit link that failed with a file-not-found error despite the script_phase running before compile. Working redirect: a Podfile pre_install hook (fetch at pod-install time, before CocoaPods writes search paths) -- verified end-to-end with otool -L showing BareKit actually linked. Recorded for the future E3 compat podspec design. All experiment files confined to the scratchpad.

title: FEAS-4: TCC Local Network verification — traffic evidence plus Apple-rule mapping (top sim-invisible risk)
description:
CONTEXT: Epic flutter_pear-ovt.1 feasibility check 4 — CEO CRITICAL. Since iOS 14, unicast traffic to local-network addresses triggers the Local Network TCC prompt (requires NSLocalNetworkUsageDescription in the consumer's Info.plist); multicast/broadcast additionally requires the com.apple.developer.networking.multicast entitlement. If Hyperswarm holepunching to a LAN peer trips TCC and the user denies the prompt, same-Wi-Fi peer connections silently fail — the flagship demo scenario. THE SIMULATOR DOES NOT ENFORCE TCC, so this is sim-invisible: the answer must come from traffic-pattern evidence plus Apple's documented rules, and must be recorded as the top sim-invisible risk (a single borrowed-iPhone run is the recommended NON-GATING mitigation — settled decision D9, do not gate on it). Setup available from the chat task: working sim-iOS to physical-Android chat, Android at 192.168.0.251:5555 on the same LAN as this Mac.
DO:
1. With chat connected and messages flowing, capture traffic on the Mac: sudo tcpdump -i en0 -n host 192.168.0.251 for a bounded window. If sudo is unavailable in this session, raise bd human asking the developer to run that one capture command, and proceed with step 1b meanwhile: lsof -nP -i UDP filtered to the example app's sim process to enumerate its sockets and peers.
2. Determine from the capture whether the sim app sends UDP DIRECTLY to the Android's LAN IP (local-network holepunch) or only communicates with external DHT/relay addresses.
3. Source inspection for multicast/broadcast: grep -rn for multicast, 224., 255.255.255.255, setBroadcast, and mdns across packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/node_modules/hyperswarm, node_modules/dht-rpc, node_modules/@hyperswarm, and node_modules/udx-native JS sources. Record hits or their absence.
4. Map evidence to Apple's rules and answer both questions: (a) will Hyperswarm holepunching to a LAN peer trigger the Local Network prompt on real devices — YES/NO from the direct-LAN-unicast evidence; (b) is the multicast entitlement ever needed — YES/NO from step 3.
5. bd update flutter_pear-ovt.1 --append-notes with a note starting FEAS-TCC: containing both answers, the tcpdump/lsof/grep evidence summary, and this caveat verbatim: the simulator does not enforce TCC, so this is the top sim-invisible risk; physical-iPhone verification is recommended and non-gating.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Traffic evidence establishes whether direct sim-to-LAN-peer UDP occurs during chat.
- Multicast/broadcast usage across the Hyperswarm stack grepped and answered.
- Both answers (prompt-triggering YES/NO; multicast entitlement YES/NO) recorded with evidence.
- The sim-invisible caveat recorded verbatim.
- The epic note begins FEAS-TCC:.
AUTO-VALIDATION:
- The capture command plus a packet count between the Mac and 192.168.0.251 during the chat window pasted into the note (or the lsof socket table if tcpdump was developer-run).
- The step 3 grep commands run with outputs saved — zero hits is a valid recordable outcome.
- bd show flutter_pear-ovt.1 | grep -c FEAS-TCC prints at least 1.
REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).
3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.
CONSTRAINTS: record-only feasibility (Info.plist keys, docs, and doctor checks land in later epics); do not relitigate D9 (no physical-device gate); all P2P logic stays in JS — no protocol changes to influence the answer.
close_reason: Both feasibility questions answered with definitive source-level evidence: (a) YES, Hyperswarm/hyperdht defaults to gathering and sharing each peer's LAN-local IP addresses during connection handshake (hyperdht/lib/connect.js + holepuncher.js's subnet-matching logic) -- this WILL trigger the iOS Local Network TCC prompt on real devices. (b) NO, multicast entitlement never needed -- zero multicast/broadcast/mDNS usage anywhere in the Hyperswarm dependency stack. Live tcpdump unavailable (no sudo); lsof fallback attempted but structurally inconclusive for this connectionless-UDP stack. Full evidence in flutter_pear-ovt.1's FEAS-TCC note, including the required sim-invisible caveat.

title: T2: hot-restart reattach-or-kill on iOS, including a restart during a message burst
description:
CONTEXT: Epic flutter_pear-ovt.1 T2 — hot-restart reattach-or-kill must work on iOS (a non-negotiable repo convention: never require an app reinstall). Dart side: BareWorklet.start detects an existing native worklet and reattaches — see packages/flutter_pear_bare/lib/src/bare_worklet.dart (reattached flag, lines 96-123; generationId stale-frame dropping, lines 198-215; machinery from issue flutter_pear-3vh). This only works if the native host keeps the worklet in static scope across Flutter engine restarts and reports reattached true with an unchanged generationId — the Kotlin reference is packages/flutter_pear_bare/android/src/main/kotlin/tech/loable/flutter_pear_bare/FlutterPearBarePlugin.kt (Companion-static worklet and generation semantics, comments around lines 26-74). The throwaway Swift host from T0 returned reattached false and generationId 1 unconditionally; this task makes its reattach semantics real enough for the spike (still throwaway, still in the example Runner). The generation/stale-exit machinery has NO iOS automated coverage, so a hot-restart-during-message-burst check is explicitly required (Eng2 #8). Android peer at adb 192.168.0.251:5555.
DO:
1. Extend the throwaway Swift host: hold the Worklet and IPC in static scope; on start, if a worklet is already running return reattached true with the existing generationId, else boot fresh and bump generationId — mirroring the Kotlin Companion semantics. Have the example app debugPrint the reattached value returned by BareWorklet.start (throwaway log line).
2. Launch on the sim with flutter run -d <sim UDID> --pid-file /tmp/fp_sim.pid; join the chat topic with the Android peer connected.
3. Trigger a hot restart scriptably: kill -USR2 $(cat /tmp/fp_sim.pid). Confirm from logs that start reattached (reattached true) and chat still works afterward without reinstall.
4. Burst test: have the Android peer send a rapid burst of messages (adb shell input driven loop, or manual rapid sends), fire kill -USR2 mid-burst, then verify no crash, no stale-generation frames surfacing in the new isolate (no exceptions or garbled state in the console), and messaging still works.
5. Verify the kill path once: terminate, then a fresh start boots cleanly.
6. bd update flutter_pear-ovt.1 --append-notes with a note starting T2-HOTRESTART-EVIDENCE: (reattach log lines, burst outcome, kill-path outcome).
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Hot restart on iOS reattaches to the running worklet (reattached true observed in logs); no app reinstall needed.
- Chat functional after restart with the same Android peer.
- MANUAL: hot restart during an active message burst causes no crash and no stale-generation leakage; app remains functional, judged from logs plus on-screen state.
- Terminate-then-fresh-start path verified once.
- Epic note T2-HOTRESTART-EVIDENCE: recorded.
AUTO-VALIDATION:
- kill -USR2 $(cat /tmp/fp_sim.pid) followed by grep of the flutter run log for the reattached debug line shows reattached true.
- adb -s 192.168.0.251:5555 shell echo ok prints ok before the burst test.
REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).
3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.
CONSTRAINTS: hot reload/restart correctness is a non-negotiable convention (reattach or cleanly kill); all P2P logic in JS; the throwaway host stays in the example Runner — no packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios formalization.
notes: REFINEMENT (same pattern as this session's earlier spike-epic corrections): this task's premise -- 'the throwaway Swift host from T0... this task makes its reattach semantics real enough for the spike (still throwaway, still in the example Runner)' and CONSTRAINTS' 'no packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios formalization' -- is now OBSOLETE. packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/flutter_pear_bare/Sources/flutter_pear_bare/FlutterPearBarePlugin.swift is a REAL, formalized 362-line host (built as part of epic 3's CocoaPods/Xcode integration, confirmed present this session alongside 3.6/3.7's podspec work) that ALREADY implements everything DO step 1 asks for: static-scope worklet/IPC across engine restarts (mirroring the Kotlin Companion pattern exactly), a workletGeneration counter, a reattached boolean returned from startWorklet, suspend/resume/terminate, and the onWorkletExit crash backstop. packages/flutter_pear_example/lib/main.dart line 295 already debugPrints reattached=${pear.worklet.reattached} (also already satisfies DO step 1's ask, no throwaway line needed). Proceeding directly to DO steps 2-6 (the actual hot-restart/burst/terminate VALIDATION against the real host) using an Android-emulator substitute for the physical Android peer, per the same developer sign-off already used for flutter_pear-ovt.1.8 ('close the hardware dependencies since testing will be emulator only').
close_reason: Real hot-restart reattach validated against the REAL (already-formalized, not throwaway) Swift host on an iOS simulator + Android-emulator peer, developer-authorized substitute per the same sign-off as flutter_pear-ovt.1.8. 3/3 hot restarts (incl. one mid message-burst) correctly reattached (reattached=true, no reinstall, zero crashes/exceptions), chat confirmed functional post-restart with real received messages, terminate-then-fresh-start verified (correct reattached=false on a genuinely fresh boot). Full evidence in flutter_pear-ovt.1's T2-HOTRESTART-EVIDENCE note.

title: T2/D11: iOS backgrounding linger investigation — record the lifecycle decision from evidence
description:
CONTEXT: Epic flutter_pear-ovt.1, design question D11 — decide iOS suspend behavior FROM EVIDENCE, not up front. Today packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/lifecycle.dart arms a 20-second linger timer after the app backgrounds (PearLifecycleDefaults.linger at line 28, constructor default at line 54) and then calls onSuspend, which drives the worklet suspend RPC. That value is Android-tuned; iOS may freeze the process before a foreground-isolate timer fires, so suspend would never reach the worklet. Question: on the iOS simulator with a running worklet, does the Dart linger timer fire after backgrounding, and does the suspend call complete? Caveat that MUST be recorded verbatim: the simulator validates the suspend/resume code path only, not real iOS background suspension or socket-kill behavior — hardware re-verification joins the physical-iPhone follow-up. If the measurement is unclean, the ALREADY-CHOSEN candidate fix (record the decision only, do NOT implement) is native-side suspend in the future Swift host — BareKit suspendWithLinger called on applicationDidEnterBackground inside a short beginBackgroundTask — and it MUST honor the Dart-configured PearLifecycle(linger:) value passed over the channel (DX2 #52: a public API override must not be silently bypassed).
DO:
1. Add throwaway debugPrint timestamps in the example app around PearLifecycle onSuspend and onResume (working-tree-only instrumentation).
2. On the booted sim with Pear.start() done (reuse the chat setup if convenient), background the app: xcrun simctl launch booted com.apple.mobilesafari (foregrounding Safari backgrounds the example), with a log capture running (flutter run console or xcrun simctl spawn booted log stream filtered to the app).
3. Measure whether onSuspend fires roughly 20s after backgrounding and whether the suspend RPC completes (worklet acknowledgment visible in logs). Repeat three times.
4. Foreground the app again; verify onResume fires and the worklet resumes (a chat message still flows).
5. If the example exposes a configurable linger, run one pass with a 2s linger; otherwise note that only the default was measured.
6. Write the D11 decision from the evidence — either (a) the Dart-side linger works on the sim, keep it, revisit on hardware, or (b) unclean: adopt native-side suspendWithLinger in the Swift host epic, honoring the Dart linger value over the channel. Record via bd update flutter_pear-ovt.1 --append-notes, note starting D11-DECISION:, containing the decision, the three timestamped runs, the resume result, and the sim-only caveat sentence.
7. Revert the throwaway instrumentation (or leave it clearly marked and uncommitted).
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- At least three background/foreground measurement runs logged with timestamps.
- The does-suspend-fire question answered concretely for the simulator, including whether the suspend RPC completed.
- Resume path verified once.
- D11-DECISION: note recorded with decision, evidence, sim-only caveat, and the DX2 #52 requirement restated if the native fix is chosen.
- No native suspend implementation written (decision only).
AUTO-VALIDATION:
- The log capture showing the backgrounding timestamp and the onSuspend timestamp (or its absence after more than 30s) is saved and summarized in the note.
- bd show flutter_pear-ovt.1 | grep -c D11-DECISION prints at least 1.
REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).
3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.
CONSTRAINTS: Futures for calls and broadcast Streams for events (instrumentation follows existing patterns); do not relitigate sim-tier validation; decision recorded here, implementation deferred to the Swift host epic.
notes: AUTO-VALIDATION: three background/foreground rounds logged with wall-clock timestamps (see epic D11-DECISION note for full detail) -- Round 1: paused 22:37:33.662 -> onSuspend 22:38:11.089 (~37s background, timer frozen throughout, fired on foreground-return not during background). Round 2: paused 22:38:47.078 -> onSuspend 22:39:52.107 (~65s background). Round 3: paused 22:40:05.986 -> onSuspend 22:40:29.067 (~23s background). All identical pattern regardless of duration. bd show flutter_pear-ovt.1 | grep -c D11-DECISION -> 1. melos run analyze: clean (4/4). flutter_pear flutter test: 204/204 after reverting the throwaway lifecycle.dart debugPrint instrumentation (git diff on lifecycle.dart is empty).
close_reason: D11 decided from evidence: UNCLEAN. Dart-side linger Timer freezes entirely while the app is backgrounded on the iOS simulator and only catches up (firing onSuspend immediately before onResume) the instant the app returns to foreground -- confirmed across 3 rounds with background durations of 23s/37s/65s, all identical. The worklet is never actually suspended during real backgrounding today; native-side suspendWithLinger in the future Swift host (honoring the Dart linger value, DX2 #52) is the recorded fix, deferred to the E3 host epic. Sim-only caveat recorded verbatim. No implementation written, instrumentation reverted, quality gates green.

title: T2: cross-platform chat smoke — simulator-iOS and physical-Android, both directions
description:
CONTEXT: Epic flutter_pear-ovt.1 T2 smoke gate: simulator-iOS and physical-Android exchange chat messages BOTH directions over a real Hyperswarm topic. The example chat screen derives a topic from typed text via PearCrypto.unsafeTopicFromString (packages/flutter_pear_example/lib/main.dart around lines 270-280) — typing the same room string on both devices lands them on one fixed topic. The physical Android device historically lives at adb 192.168.0.251:5555; per repo CLI corrections install the DEBUG apk: adb -s 192.168.0.251:5555 install -r -s build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/app-debug.apk. The iOS side is the same example app on the booted sim using the throwaway Swift host and real bundle from prior tasks. The simulator shares the Mac's network stack, so real DHT traffic works.
DO:
1. adb connect 192.168.0.251:5555; cd packages/flutter_pear_example && flutter build apk --debug; install with the command above; launch via adb shell am start using the applicationId from packages/flutter_pear_example/android/app/build.gradle.kts.
2. Run the example on the sim with flutter run -d <sim UDID from PREREQ-EVIDENCE> keeping the console attached for logs.
3. Join the SAME room string on both sides (Android via adb shell input text and input tap where scriptable; on-device manual interaction is an acceptable residual — the device is local to the developer).
4. Send a uniquely identifiable message from iOS; confirm arrival on Android via adb logcat and/or on-screen. Send a different unique message from Android; confirm arrival on iOS via the flutter run console or xcrun simctl log stream and/or on-screen.
5. bd update flutter_pear-ovt.1 --append-notes with a note starting T2-CHAT-EVIDENCE: containing both message strings, timestamps, direction-by-direction confirmation, and connection-state transitions observed.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Both devices join the same fixed Hyperswarm topic and reach connected state.
- A message sent from sim-iOS is received on the physical Android, with logged evidence.
- A message sent from the physical Android is received on sim-iOS, with logged evidence.
- MANUAL: on-screen receipt visually confirmed on both devices if log evidence is ambiguous.
- Epic note T2-CHAT-EVIDENCE: recorded covering both directions.
AUTO-VALIDATION:
- adb -s 192.168.0.251:5555 shell echo ok prints ok (device reachable).
- adb -s 192.168.0.251:5555 logcat -d | grep <unique iOS-sent string> shows the message arrived on Android.
- Bounded log capture on the Mac (flutter run console grep, or timeout 30 xcrun simctl spawn booted log stream with a predicate on the app process) shows the Android-sent unique string arriving on iOS.
REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).

4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.
CONSTRAINTS: all P2P logic stays in JS — if chat fails, the bug is in host wiring, bundle, or addons, never fixed by reimplementing protocol in Dart; do not relitigate sim-tier validation (sim-iOS to physical-Android IS the accepted v0.2 tier).
notes: AUTO-VALIDATION: adb -s 192.168.0.251:5555 shell echo ok -> ok (device reachable, confirming the FIRST auto-validation bullet). The remaining two bullets (Android receiving iOS's message via logcat grep, and iOS receiving Android's message) cannot be satisfied: Android-side Pear.start() itself fails before ever joining the swarm (worklet_start_failed / libnativehelper.so dlopen failure specific to this BYD DiLink head unit's ROM -- confirmed reproducible across 2 runs + a clean uninstall/reinstall, and confirmed NOT a packaging issue via unzip -l on the APK showing all 13 .so files present and correctly named for arm64-v8a). iOS side ran cleanly and simply timed out waiting for a peer that never joined. Flagged bd human -- see the T2-CHAT-EVIDENCE note on the parent epic for full detail and the developer decision this needs (borrow real Android hardware / accept an emulator substitute with explicit sign-off / defer). Not closing: the physical-Android acceptance criterion is not met, and closing with a false 'done' would misrepresent the epic's actual T2 status.
close_reason: Real cross-platform chat smoke test completed using an Android emulator as an explicit, developer-authorized substitute for the unavailable physical BYD device (its libnativehelper.so dlopen failure remains unresolved/out of scope). Both directions confirmed with logged + on-screen evidence across 3 send attempts; full timeline in flutter_pear-ovt.1's T2-CHAT-EVIDENCE note. Connection establishment is real but slow on emulator networking (documented for future runners). One side finding investigated and closed as not-a-bug (flutter_pear-6ic).

title: T1 exit: real pear-end bundle boots on the iOS simulator with passing version handshake
description:
CONTEXT: Epic flutter_pear-ovt.1 T1 exit gate: the REAL pear-end bundle boots on the iOS simulator and the session nonce/version handshake passes. Inputs from prior tasks: the iOS-capable committed bundle at packages/flutter_pear/assets/pear-end.bundle (regenerated by :pack, baked version matching kPearEndBundleVersion in packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/bundle_version.dart), addon xcframeworks in the gitignored .spike/addons/, and the throwaway Swift host in packages/flutter_pear_example/ios/Runner. Handshake mechanics: Pear.start (packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/pear.dart lines 65 and 110-133) calls PearMethod.attachInfo; a mismatch throws with PearErrorCode.bundleVersionMismatch. Bundle asset resolution: the Android Kotlin host uses FlutterAssets.getAssetFilePathBySubpath with assets/pear-end.bundle and package flutter_pear (see the comment at packages/flutter_pear/bin/pack.dart line 18); the Swift equivalent is FlutterDartProject lookupKey(forAsset: assets/pear-end.bundle, fromPackage: flutter_pear) then Bundle.main.path(forResource:).
DO:
1. Extend the T0 shim (Package.swift shim or local pod in packages/flutter_pear_example/ios) to embed and sign every .spike/addons/*.xcframework alongside BareKit so Bare's addon resolver loads them at runtime (naming and embedding per the conventions recorded in the addon-linking task's FEAS-BARELINK-IOS note and react-native-bare-kit's ios/link.mjs).
2. Switch the throwaway Swift host's start handler to load the real flutter_pear bundle asset via the lookupKey path above instead of the spike bundle.
3. Add packages/flutter_pear_example/integration_test/ios_real_bundle_test.dart (TO BE CREATED): run Pear.start() and assert it completes without throwing (specifically not bundleVersionMismatch) and that Pear reports a started worklet. Gate or skip the old spike test (its bundle is no longer wired).
4. Run on the booted sim. If boot fails with addon resolve errors, capture diagnostics (xcrun simctl spawn booted log stream filtered to the app process, or the flutter run console) and iterate on step 1 naming/embedding — failure evidence also goes into the notes.
5. bd update flutter_pear-ovt.1 --append-notes with a note starting T1-EVIDENCE: (Pear.start success, observed bundleVersion, embedded addon list).
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- The real iOS-capable pear-end bundle boots inside BareKit on the sim with all native addons resolving.
- Pear.start() succeeds; attach.info handshake passes with bundleVersion equal to kPearEndBundleVersion.
- The integration test proving it passes on the sim.
- packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios still does not exist.
- Epic note T1-EVIDENCE: recorded.
AUTO-VALIDATION:
- export PATH="$HOME/development/flutter/bin:$PATH" && cd packages/flutter_pear_example && flutter test integration_test/ios_real_bundle_test.dart -d <sim UDID from PREREQ-EVIDENCE> exits 0.
- test -d packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios; echo $? prints 1.
- Mutation spot-check: temporarily edit kPearEndBundleVersion in packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/bundle_version.dart to a wrong string and re-run the integration test — it must fail with bundleVersionMismatch; revert.
REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).
3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.
CONSTRAINTS: all P2P logic in JS inside the worklet; committed artifacts versioned like source (bundle and bundle_version.dart move together, uncommitted for review); no host formalization — the single flutter_pear_bare package's iOS side is a later epic.
notes: AUTO-VALIDATION: flutter test integration_test/ios_real_bundle_test.dart -d B2B07E72-AF61-4059-895C-EF4A627A02CB exits 0. test -d packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios -> absent (exit 1). Mutation spot-check: kPearEndBundleVersion temporarily set to a wrong string -> test failed immediately with BUNDLE_VERSION_MISMATCH (not a timeout, proving the handshake genuinely validates); reverted, re-passed. melos run analyze: clean 4/4 (fixed one pre-existing issue along the way: testWidgets' skip parameter wants bool not String in this Flutter version -- moved the reason to a code comment). flutter_pear flutter test: 204/204. flutter_pear_example flutter test: 41/41.
close_reason: T1 exit gate: the real, committed pear-end.bundle (all 12 native addons + BareKit embedded via BareKitShim's per-addon SPM binaryTargets) boots on the iOS simulator and Pear.start()'s attach.info version handshake passes. Root cause of the multi-hour hang: our Swift host never passed Bare.argv[0] (index.js's storage-dir guard throws synchronously, and Bare.exit() afterward doesn't visibly close BareIPC's pipe, wedging the worklet thread silently) -- fixed by passing the app's Library directory as the start() arguments, matching Android's filesDir contract. Caught and fixed a gitignore gap (addons/ symlinks) before closing. All quality gates green.

title: T1: extend pack.dart to emit an iOS-capable bundle; record multi-host and determinism feasibility
description:
CONTEXT: Epic flutter_pear-ovt.1 T1 second half plus feasibility checks 1 and 2. packages/flutter_pear/bin/pack.dart function buildBundle (line 257) invokes bare-pack with the args --preset android at line 269; the doc comment at lines 246-256 records that this preset is shorthand for --linked plus four android --host flags. The committed bundle is packages/flutter_pear/assets/pear-end.bundle (a committed, versioned artifact); :pack also bakes a bundle version and writes packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/bundle_version.dart (kPearEndBundleVersion), which Pear.start's attach.info handshake checks (packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/pear.dart lines 110-133). DECISION already made (Eng2 #3): PREFER one multi-host bundle — Flutter assets are platform-unconditional, so per-platform bundles would ship BOTH bundles to BOTH platforms (~2MB each) and falsify the changelog's no-Android-change promise. FEASIBILITY 1: does bare-pack accept a combined android+ios host list? FEASIBILITY 2: is bare-pack output deterministic (same inputs, byte-identical output)? The future CRITICAL pack regression test depends on that answer; the fallback assertion is compare-vs-committed-artifacts (Eng2 #7).
DO:
1. In buildBundle, replace --preset android with explicit flags: --linked --host android-arm --host android-arm64 --host android-ia32 --host android-x64 --host ios-arm64 --host ios-arm64-simulator. Update the doc comment to stay accurate.
2. FEASIBILITY 1: run the build. If bare-pack rejects combined hosts or the output demonstrably lacks either platform's addon resolution entries, record NO and implement the documented fallback (a second per-platform bundle output) noting the consequences (both bundles ship to both platforms; APK delta measured in a later epic). Otherwise record YES.
3. FEASIBILITY 2: build twice into two temp paths with identical inputs; cmp the bytes. Record YES (byte-identical) or NO with the diff cause if identifiable, and note the Eng2 #7 fallback if NO.
4. export PATH="$HOME/development/flutter/bin:$PATH" && cd packages/flutter_pear && dart run flutter_pear:pack — regenerates assets/pear-end.bundle, lib/src/bundle_version.dart, THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES, and Android jniLibs consistently. Leave all regenerated artifacts uncommitted.
5. Run flutter test from packages/flutter_pear (pack_test and friends must pass).
6. bd update flutter_pear-ovt.1 --append-notes with two notes starting FEAS-MULTIHOST: and FEAS-DETERMINISM:, each YES/NO plus command evidence.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- pack.dart emits an iOS-capable bundle (ios-arm64 + ios-arm64-simulator) while keeping all four Android hosts.
- FEAS-MULTIHOST: recorded with evidence; fallback implemented with consequences recorded only if NO.
- FEAS-DETERMINISM: recorded with evidence.
- dart run flutter_pear:pack exits 0; regenerated artifacts left uncommitted.
- flutter_pear test suite passes.
AUTO-VALIDATION:
- export PATH="$HOME/development/flutter/bin:$PATH" && cd packages/flutter_pear && dart run flutter_pear:pack exits 0.
- grep -c ios-arm64 packages/flutter_pear/assets/pear-end.bundle prints at least 1 AND grep -c android-arm64 packages/flutter_pear/assets/pear-end.bundle prints at least 1 (per-platform fallback: each bundle greps for its own platform instead).
- cd packages/flutter_pear && flutter test exits 0.
- bd show flutter_pear-ovt.1 | grep -c FEAS-DETERMINISM prints at least 1.
REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).
3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.
CONSTRAINTS: committed artifacts are versioned like source — the bundle and bundle_version.dart must be regenerated together, never out of sync, and a fresh checkout must never need :pack to build; all P2P logic stays in JS (pack.dart only packages it); do not relitigate the multi-host-preferred or committed-artifact decisions; every touched public symbol stays documented.
notes: AUTO-VALIDATION: dart run flutter_pear:pack exits 0 (wrote assets/pear-end.bundle, jniLibs, THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES, NOTICE). grep -c ios-arm64/android-arm64 on the bundle both -> 0 (documented as a caveat on the epic -- bare-pack's manifest format never embeds literal host/arch strings for linked addons; the addons array's 24 entries, 12 .framework + 12 lib*.so, is the real evidence both platforms resolved correctly). flutter test in packages/flutter_pear: 204/204 pass. melos run analyze: clean. bd show flutter_pear-ovt.1 | grep -c FEAS-DETERMINISM -> 1.
close_reason: buildBundle now targets android-arm/arm64/ia32/x64 + ios-arm64/ios-arm64-simulator in one bare-pack invocation. FEAS-MULTIHOST: YES (24-entry addons array proves both platforms resolved -- literal arm64-string grep doesn't apply to this bundle format, documented). FEAS-DETERMINISM: YES (byte-identical rebuild). 204/204 flutter_pear tests pass, melos analyze clean. Regenerated artifacts left uncommitted for developer review.

title: T1: link pear-end native addons for ios-arm64 + ios-arm64-simulator; record bare-link iOS feasibility
description:
CONTEXT: Epic flutter_pear-ovt.1 T1 first half. The real pear-end bundle eagerly requires native addons at boot, so they must be linked for ios-arm64 (device) and ios-arm64-simulator before the real bundle can boot (Eng2 correction 2). Existing Android pipeline: packages/flutter_pear/bin/pack.dart, function linkNativeAddons (line 103), runs bare-link from packages/flutter_pear/pear-end with repeated --host flags (nativeAddonAbis map at line 56: android-arm64, android-x64) and harvests .so files. bare-link is installed globally. FEASIBILITY QUESTION (answer must be recorded): does bare-link support iOS hosts and emit usable output? FALLBACK if not: the addon npm packages under packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/node_modules already ship prebuilds/ios-arm64 and prebuilds/ios-arm64-simulator dirs (verified for sodium-native and udx-native; enumerate all) — harvest those and structure xcframeworks the way react-native-bare-kit does in ios/link.mjs (github.com/holepunchto/react-native-bare-kit); the fallback still owns xcframework structuring, addon registration/loading names (Bare's addon resolver naming), install names (install_name_tool), and embedding requirements. title: T0 exit: hydrate example iOS runner, hand-embed BareKit, throwaway Swift host, RPC ping on simulator
description:
CONTEXT: Epic flutter_pear-ovt.1 T0 exit gate — prove BareKit + IPC + rpc ping on the iOS simulator BEFORE any packaging/host investment. packages/flutter_pear_example has NO ios/ directory today. The platform-neutral Dart side is packages/flutter_pear_bare/lib/src/bare_worklet.dart: class BareWorklet uses MethodChannel flutter_pear_bare/control (start returns a map with reattached and generationId keys, lines 139-151; also suspend/resume/terminate) and BasicMessageChannel flutter_pear_bare/ipc with StandardMessageCodec (lines 75-87). Behavioral reference: the 326-line Kotlin host packages/flutter_pear_bare/android/src/main/kotlin/tech/loable/flutter_pear_bare/FlutterPearBarePlugin.kt. Swift usage reference for BareKit Worklet boot + IPC: github.com/holepunchto/react-native-bare-kit ios sources (same authors, bare-kit v2.3.0). The Swift host here is THROWAWAY and lives in the example Runner — creating packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios is forbidden (formalization is a later epic). Inputs: .spike/BareKit.xcframework (repo root, gitignored) and packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/spike/ios-spike.bundle. Sim UDID: see the PREREQ-EVIDENCE note on flutter_pear-ovt.1.

Implement start (boot a BareKit Worklet from the spike bundle file resolved via FlutterDartProject lookupKey(forAsset:); return reattached false, generationId 1), send (bytes to worklet IPC), an IPC read loop relaying frames to Dart, terminate, and no-op suspend/resume.

title: T0: build a minimal IPC-only iOS spike bundle (zero native addons)
description:
CONTEXT: Epic flutter_pear-ovt.1, T0 (Eng2 correction 2 — this sequencing is load-bearing). The committed bundle packages/flutter_pear/assets/pear-end.bundle CANNOT boot on iOS: packages/flutter_pear/bin/pack.dart line 269 builds it with bare-pack preset android, and packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/index.js eagerly requires native-backed modules at startup (hyperswarm at line 20; corestore, hyperdrive, sodium-native, udx-native, rocksdb transitively). T0 therefore proves BareKit + IPC + rpc ping with a MINIMAL spike bundle containing no native addons. Frame protocol: every IPC frame is a 1-byte discriminator (PearFrameType.json) followed by one UTF-8 JSON body; requests {"id","m","p"}, responses {"id","ok"} or {"id","err"} — see packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/rpc.dart (class PearRpc line 47, frame notes lines 16-25) and the top-of-file comment in pear-end/index.js. PearRpc health-probes method attach.info (PearMethod.attachInfo, packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/schema.dart line 55). bare-pack is installed globally per the repo toolchain.
DO:
1. Create packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/spike/ios-spike.js (TO BE CREATED): copy the IPC framing helpers from pear-end/index.js (send, writeFramed, the BareKit IPC read loop and JSON frame parsing) and implement exactly two behaviors: respond to attach.info mirroring the response payload shape index.js uses for that method (grep index.js for the attach.info handler) with bundleVersion hardcoded to the string ios-spike, and answer every other method with an err response. Require nothing beyond Bare builtins — zero native addons.
2. From packages/flutter_pear/pear-end run: bare-pack --linked --host ios-arm64 --host ios-arm64-simulator --out spike/ios-spike.bundle spike/ios-spike.js

title: Step 0 prereqs: verify Xcode + iOS simulator runtimes and Flutter SPM-default status
description:
CONTEXT: First task of epic flutter_pear-ovt.1 (E1: iOS spike + feasibility) in the flutter_pear melos monorepo (v0.1 shipped Android-only; v0.2 adds iOS). Before any iOS code, two plan prerequisites must be verified and recorded on the epic issue: (a) this Mac has Xcode plus at least one iOS simulator runtime; (b) the plan's SPM-first bet rests on the landscape claim that Flutter 3.44 made Swift Package Manager the default for iOS plugin deps — verify against https://docs.flutter.dev/packages-and-plugins/swift-package-manager/for-plugin-authors and record the minimum Flutter version for the SPM plugin path. The repo pins Flutter SDK >=3.24. Flutter lives at $HOME/development/flutter/bin.
DO:
1. Run xcodebuild -version and xcrun simctl list runtimes. If Xcode or all iOS runtimes are missing, flag with bd human on this issue stating exactly what is missing — do not install anything silently.
2. Run xcrun simctl list devices available, pick an available iPhone simulator, and note its UDID (later spike tasks use it).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Xcode version and at least one installed iOS simulator runtime recorded in the epic notes.
- A concrete simulator UDID chosen for the spike is recorded.
- SPM-default question answered with the docs URL as source, including the minimum Flutter version for SPM plugin authors.
- The epic note begins with PREREQ-EVIDENCE:.
- If a prerequisite is missing, bd human was raised instead of guessing.
AUTO-VALIDATION:

- export PATH="$HOME/development/flutter/bin:$PATH" && flutter --version exits 0 showing Dart >=3.5.
- bd show flutter_pear-ovt.1 | grep -c PREREQ-EVIDENCE prints at least 1.

3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.

title: v0.2 E3: Swift worklet host + SPM/CocoaPods packaging
description:
Swift worklet host in flutter_pear_bare + SPM-first packaging. Plan: ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-flutter_pear/andrewloable-main-design-20260706-031805.md (steps 3-4, Eng review 3B/4A, Eng2 #4, CEO rows 14-15, DX2 #52/#55). Depends on the spike epic (T0/T1 must have passed; D11 lifecycle decision and preflight-ordering answer must exist).

CONTEXT AN IMPLEMENTER NEEDS: flutter_pear_bare is the low-level package (keep it SMALL — it is the burnout-risk core): worklet lifecycle + raw binary IPC only. The Dart side (packages/flutter_pear_bare/lib/src/bare_worklet.dart) is platform-neutral and defines the platform-channel contract; the Android host is packages/flutter_pear_bare/android/ (Kotlin). The iOS job is a Swift mirror of that SAME contract — read the Kotlin host and bare_worklet.dart first and change NEITHER's semantics. react-native-bare-kit's ios/ directory is the reference implementation for embedding BareKit on iOS. No packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/ exists today.

TASKS:
1. Swift host (packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/): worklet start from bundle bytes/asset, binary IPC in both directions, suspend/resume/terminate, and native crash observation feeding the same onCrash event surface the Kotlin host feeds. Match method/event channel names and payload encoding EXACTLY (session nonce + version handshake must pass unmodified — kPearEndBundleVersion, attach.info).
2. Hot-restart parity: reattach-or-kill on iOS using the same generation/stale-exit machinery contract (flutter_pear-3vh implemented it; the Dart side already handles it — the host must detect an existing worklet after Dart hot-restart and support reattach or clean kill; never require reinstall).
3. iOS storage roots (Eng2 #4, CRITICAL): worklet storage base = Application Support (NEVER Documents). Set isExcludedFromBackup=true on the pear-corestore and pear-bulk directories — an iCloud restore of Hypercore writer keys onto a second device FORKS cores (protocol corruption, not a UX nicety). Files-app-visible outputs are a SEPARATE Documents subtree owned by the app/demo layer, never the worklet.
4. Lifecycle: implement whatever the spike's D11 evidence decided. If native-side suspend is needed: BareKit suspendWithLinger on didEnterBackground inside a short beginBackgroundTask, and it MUST honor the Dart-configured PearLifecycle(linger:) value (DX2 #52 — the existing Dart 20s default at packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/lifecycle.dart:54 is Android-tuned). Add the lifecycle state diagram as a comment in BOTH hosts (eng 4A) so they cannot drift silently.
5. SPM packaging (CEO rows 14-15): the plugin ships Package.swift (GENERATED by :pack from barekit-pin.json — see the pack epic; never hand-edit) with path binaryTargets for committed addon xcframeworks + URL binaryTarget(url:checksum:) for the repacked BareKit. Follow docs.flutter.dev swift-package-manager/for-plugin-authors. Record the minimum Flutter and iOS deployment versions in both manifests and the compatibility table.
6. CocoaPods compat podspec: fetches BareKit via script_phase at BUILD time using barekit-pin.json checksums. prepare_command is FORBIDDEN — CocoaPods never runs it for :path-installed pods and Flutter installs ALL plugin pods as :path development pods (CocoaPods#2187). Shape per the spike's CocoaPods external-consumer feasibility answer.
7. Build-time preflight (eng 3B, D18): a check that BareKit + addon xcframeworks are present/checksum-valid, emitting an ACTIONABLE error (what is missing, the exact fix command/URL) before the developer hits a raw linker error. Placement (script phase vs plugin build tool) per the spike's preflight-ordering answer; if nothing can run pre-resolution, the troubleshooting doc carries that path instead.
8. Pear.platformInfo (DX2 #55, additive Dart getter in packages/flutter_pear): ships BOTH fields. backgroundExecution: enum foregroundOnly|bestEffort (iOS: foregroundOnly unless D11 evidence justifies otherwise; Android: bestEffort). validationTier: pinned semantics (iOS: simulator; Android: device). Wire native values through attach.info or a dedicated call; update flutter_pear_test fake to expose the same getter (test parity is asserted in the tests epic).

ACCEPTANCE: example app builds and runs on iOS sim via SPM with zero manual Xcode edits; same via the compat podspec in an external (non-monorepo) consumer; handshake + hot-restart + suspend/resume pass on sim; storage lands in Application Support with backup-exclusion verifiable (xcrun simctl + ls metadata or unit assertion); preflight produces its actionable error when BareKit is deliberately absent; Pear.platformInfo returns both fields on iOS, Android, and the fake.

title: v0.2 E2: pack tooling, BareKit repack, pear-end symlink filter
description:
Pack tooling, artifacts and pear-end changes. Plan: ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-flutter_pear/andrewloable-main-design-20260706-031805.md (step 3 partial, Eng review 1A/2A, Eng2 #1/#3/#5/#7-#10, DX2 #48/#50). Depends on the spike epic's feasibility answers (multi-host bundle, bare-link iOS, determinism).

CONTEXT AN IMPLEMENTER NEEDS: packages/flutter_pear/bin/pack.dart is the maintainer-only tool (dart run flutter_pear:pack) that builds assets/pear-end.bundle via bare-pack, links native addons via bare-link into committed artifacts (Android: flutter_pear_bare/android/src/main/jniLibs/<abi>/*.so — the k2y decision: consumer-build-time resolution silently shipped broken APKs), and generates THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES. Committed artifacts are versioned like source; a fresh checkout must never need :pack to build. Android BareKit itself is NOT committed — it is a checksum-pinned Gradle fetch at consumer build time (bareKitVersion 2.3.0 + SHA256 in flutter_pear_bare/android/build.gradle).

TASKS:
1. iOS bundle emission (from spike): multi-host bundle preferred; if per-platform forced, :pack regenerates both ATOMICALLY and asserts both baked versions equal kPearEndBundleVersion (Eng2 #8), the changelog wording is amended, the APK delta measured/disclosed, and the CRITICAL regression extends to the Android ASSET SET.
2. linkNativeAddons for iOS -> committed packages/flutter_pear_bare/ios/addons/*.xcframework, device arm64 + simulator arm64 ONLY (D21). Use spike's chosen mechanism (bare-link or harvest-and-structure).
3. BareKit repack + rehost (Eng2 #1, CRITICAL): upstream ships one 354MB multi-platform prebuilds.zip; SPM binaryTarget(url:checksum:) needs an xcframework-at-root zip. :pack gains a step: download pinned upstream zip, verify upstream SHA256, extract BareKit.xcframework (iOS slices), re-zip at archive root, upload as a release asset on THIS repo (gh release upload), and write barekit-pin.json with the TWO-LINK CHECKSUM CHAIN {bareKitVersion, upstreamUrl, upstreamSha256, repackedUrl, repackedSha256}. Supply-chain note: provenance becomes maintainer-repacked with an auditable chain.
4. Generate Package.swift from barekit-pin.json (CEO row 15): path binaryTargets for the committed addons + URL binaryTarget for the repacked BareKit. Single pin source; never hand-edit Package.swift.
5. Size guards (eng 1A + outside voice): :pack FAILS if any single committed artifact >= 95MB (GitHub hard limit is 100MB) OR the projected compressed pub archive exceeds pub.dev's ceiling; prints total committed-artifact growth delta per regen.
6. THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES regeneration covers iOS artifacts. WATCH OUT for silent-failure paths flagged by verification: pack.dart:528 _addBareKitStaticEntry soft-skips if the gradle file path is wrong (license entry silently vanishes).
7. pear-end (the ONLY pear-end changes in v0.2): (a) reject/skip symlink entries when mirroring untrusted peer drives (Eng2 #6 — VERIFIED zip-slip: mirror-drive/index.js:324-325 propagates remote symlinks and localdrive symlink() writes relative targets uncontained; a malicious peer publishes symlink /x -> ../../.. then file /x/payload -> arbitrary write in the app sandbox; DRIVE_MIRROR_TO_DISK is pear-end index.js:980). Filter before drive.mirror or wrap Localdrive. (b) Emit a TYPED WARNING EVENT when entries are rejected (DX2 #54 — a dev debugging "file never arrived" must see why) surfaced on the Dart side.

ACCEPTANCE: dart run flutter_pear:pack produces bundle(s) + ios addon xcframeworks + Package.swift + barekit-pin.json + THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES in one run; size guards demonstrably fire (test with a threshold override); repacked BareKit asset downloadable with checksums verifying end-to-end; hostile-drive mirror rejects symlink and deep-path entries with a warning event observable from Dart.

title: v0.2 E1: iOS spike + feasibility (T0-T2)
description:
Spike and feasibility: prove the iOS transport path and answer every feasibility question BEFORE packaging/host investment. Plan: ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-flutter_pear/andrewloable-main-design-20260706-031805.md (steps 0-2, T0-T2, Eng2 corrections 1-2, 11-12).

PREREQS (step 0):
- Verify Xcode + iOS simulator runtimes installed on this Mac.
- Confirm current Flutter SPM-default status and the minimum Flutter version for the SPM plugin path (landscape claims: Flutter 3.44 made SPM default; verify against docs.flutter.dev/packages-and-plugins/swift-package-manager/for-plugin-authors).

T0 — PROOF OF LIFE (sequencing is load-bearing, Eng2 #2):
- The committed pear-end bundle CANNOT boot on iOS: packages/flutter_pear/bin/pack.dart:247 builds with --preset android, and pear-end/index.js eagerly imports native-backed modules (hyperswarm, hyperdrive, sodium, udx, rocksdb) at startup. So T0 uses a MINIMAL IPC-ONLY spike bundle (no native addons) just to prove BareKit + IPC + bare-rpc ping.
- Obtain BareKit.xcframework: upstream holepunchto/bare-kit v2.3.0 releases ship ONE multi-platform prebuilds.zip (~354MB); manually extract the iOS xcframework for the spike (the productized repack lives in the pack epic).
- Hydrate the example iOS runner: cd packages/flutter_pear_example && flutter create --platforms=ios . (no ios/ dir exists today). Hand-embed BareKit (manual Xcode embed or minimal local Package.swift shim — throwaway).
- Exit: worklet boots on sim, RPC ping round-trips.

T1 — REAL BUNDLE BOOTS:
- Link the native addons for ios-arm64 (device) + ios-arm64-simulator via bare-link. FALLBACK if bare-link lacks iOS support: harvest prebuilds/ios-* from the addon npm packages under pear-end/node_modules (they exist, incl. ios-x64-simulator) and structure xcframeworks the way react-native-bare-kit's ios/link.mjs does — that fallback still owns xcframework structuring, addon registration/loading, install names, embedding.
- Extend pack.dart minimally to emit an iOS-capable bundle. PREFERRED: one multi-host bundle (bare-pack presets are just host-flag lists; verify combined android+ios hosts work — Eng2 #3; Flutter assets are platform-unconditional, so per-platform bundles would ship BOTH bundles to BOTH platforms and falsify the changelog promise). Fallback: per-platform bundles + consequences documented.
- Exit: real bundle boots on sim; session nonce/version handshake passes (kPearEndBundleVersion in lib/src/bundle_version.dart must match — attach.info handshake).

T2 — CROSS-PLATFORM CHAT SMOKE:
- Fixed Hyperswarm topic; sim-iOS <-> physical-Android (adb device at 192.168.0.251:5555 historically) chat BOTH directions.
- Lifecycle investigation (design doc D11, decide from EVIDENCE): the Dart-side 20s linger (packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/lifecycle.dart:54) is Android-tuned; measure whether iOS freezes the process before the timer fires when backgrounding the sim app. If unclean: candidate fix is native-side suspend in the Swift host (BareKit suspendWithLinger on didEnterBackground inside a short beginBackgroundTask) — and it MUST honor the Dart-configured PearLifecycle(linger:) value passed over the channel (DX2 #52).
- Hot-restart reattach-or-kill works on iOS; also manually test hot-restart during a message burst (generation/stale-exit machinery, flutter_pear-3vh, has no iOS automated coverage).

FEASIBILITY CHECKS (each produces a recorded yes/no + evidence in this epic's notes):
1. bare-pack multi-host bundle support (see T1).
2. bare-pack output determinism (same inputs -> byte-identical bundle?) — the CRITICAL pack regression test depends on the answer; fallback assertion is compare-vs-committed-artifacts (Eng2 #7).
3. Preflight ordering: does a Package.swift/podspec script phase actually run BEFORE SwiftPM resolution/link errors reach the user? If not, troubleshooting docs carry that path (D18).
4. TCC verification: does Hyperswarm holepunching to a LAN peer actually trigger the iOS Local Network permission prompt (NSLocalNetworkUsageDescription)? Is the multicast entitlement ever needed? (CEO CRITICAL — the prompt is SIM-INVISIBLE: the simulator does not enforce TCC, so this is documented as the top sim-invisible risk.)
5. CocoaPods external-consumer spike (Eng2 #12, MUST land before demo-gate work): CocoaPods resolves vendored-framework references at pod install but script_phase runs at build — build a throwaway EXTERNAL consumer app (not the melos example) consuming the podspec to prove a build-time-fetched framework links. If it fails: install-time generation or a real pod dependency. NOTE: prepare_command is NOT an option — CocoaPods never runs prepare_command for :path-installed pods (CocoaPods#2187) and Flutter installs ALL plugin pods as :path development pods.

ACCEPTANCE: T0/T1/T2 pass with logged evidence; all 5 feasibility answers recorded; D11 lifecycle decision made from evidence; no packaging/host formalization started before the relevant answer exists.
notes:
PREREQ-EVIDENCE: Xcode 26.6 (Build 17F113) installed at /Applications/Xcode_26.0.app. iOS simulator runtimes installed: iOS 26.2, iOS 26.4 (x2 builds), iOS 26.5 (4 total iOS runtimes, all available). Chosen spike simulator: iPhone 17 Pro, iOS 26.5, UDID B2B07E72-AF61-4059-895C-EF4A627A02CB (currently Shutdown, will boot on demand). Flutter 3.44.4 (Dart 3.12.2), channel stable -- meets the repo pin of >=3.24. flutter config --list shows enable-swift-package-manager as Not set (uses default). Per https://docs.flutter.dev/packages-and-plugins/swift-package-manager/for-plugin-authors: SwiftPM is the default for plugin authors as of Flutter 3.44 or later ('Ensure that you are using Flutter 3.44 or later. This enables SwiftPM by default.'). Minimum Flutter version for the SPM plugin path is 3.44 -- this repo's installed Flutter (3.44.4) already meets it, so no SDK bump is needed for the SPM-first bet. CocoaPods is NOT installed on this Mac (flutter doctor flags it); needed later for the CocoaPods external-consumer spike (1.13) and E3 packaging.
BAREKIT-ARTIFACT: downloaded holepunchto/bare-kit v2.3.0 prebuilds.zip (354.0MB), sha256 a386063fa405b0bb4967490e84745075f007f95359c9871c5b7a45c18c2f49e2 -- matches the pin in packages/flutter_pear_bare/android/build.gradle exactly. The zip ships FOUR xcframework variants: apple/ (full, Bare+JS engine, has ios-arm64 + macos-arm64_x86_64 + ios-arm64_x86_64-simulator slices), apple-javascriptcore/ (JSC-backed, smaller), darwin/ (macOS-only, no iOS slices), and apple/ has the only one with true iOS device+simulator slices. Chose in-zip path apple/BareKit.xcframework/. Extracted to .spike/BareKit.xcframework/ -- Info.plist AvailableLibraries lists 3 slices: ios-arm64 (device, arm64), ios-arm64_x86_64-simulator (simulator, arm64+x86_64), macos-arm64_x86_64 (host, unused for the iOS spike). D11-DECISION: UNCLEAN -- adopt native-side suspendWithLinger in the future Swift host (E3), honoring the Dart-configured PearLifecycle(linger:) value passed over the channel (DX2 #52 restated: a public API override must not be silently bypassed by a hardcoded native default). SIM-ONLY CAVEAT (verbatim, per the task): the simulator validates the suspend/resume code path only, not real iOS background suspension or socket-kill behavior -- hardware re-verification joins the physical-iPhone follow-up. EVIDENCE (three timestamped background/foreground rounds via throwaway debugPrint instrumentation in lifecycle.dart, reverted after -- xcrun simctl launch booted com.apple.mobilesafari to background, xcrun simctl launch booted com.example.flutterPearExample to foreground, on the booted iPhone 17 Pro / iOS 26.5 sim with a live Pear.start()): Round 1 -- paused 22:37:33.662, backgrounded for ~37s (a periodic 5s diagnostic Timer's own ticks went completely silent for the whole period -- proving Dart timer execution freezes while paused, not just PearLifecycle's), onSuspend() fired at 22:38:11.089 -- NOT ~20s after backgrounding, but IMMEDIATELY (within 1ms) of the app returning to foreground; onResume() followed ~447ms later, still inside the same foreground-return burst. Round 2 -- paused 22:38:47.078, backgrounded ~65s, onSuspend() fired at 22:39:52.107 (again immediately on foreground return, ~445ms before onResume()). Round 3 -- paused 22:40:05.986, backgrounded ~23s, onSuspend() fired at 22:40:29.067 (again immediate, ~440ms before onResume()). All three rounds: 100% consistent pattern regardless of actual background duration (23s/37s/65s all behaved identically). CONCLUSION: the pure-Dart Timer-based linger NEVER actually fires while the app is genuinely backgrounded on iOS (the whole isolate/timer queue freezes) -- it only 'catches up' the instant the app returns to the foreground, which means Pear.suspend() is effectively called AFTER the user has already returned, not during the background period. This provides ZERO of the intended benefit (releasing the P2P connection/radio while backgrounded to save battery) -- the worklet stays fully live and connected for the ENTIRE background duration on iOS today, contradicting BACKGROUND_EXECUTION.md's implied behavior. Resume path verified working correctly all 3 rounds (onResume() fires promptly, ~440-550ms after onSuspend() in the same burst). No native suspend implementation was written -- decision recorded only, per the task's own scope; melos analyze clean and flutter_pear's 204 tests still pass after reverting the debugPrint instrumentation.

Set up entirely in the session scratchpad (gem install cocoapods --user-install, no CocoaPods on this Mac before now): a throwaway BareKitFetchPod (vendored_frameworks + a before_compile script_phase copying .spike/BareKit.xcframework into the pod's own srcroot) consumed via :path by a fresh external flutter create --platforms=ios app (outside the melos workspace). BASE RECIPE RESULT: pod install succeeded with the framework absent from disk (first acceptance bullet met) and xcodebuild BUILD SUCCEEDED -- but that first green build was a FALSE POSITIVE: the Swift code used #if canImport(BareKit), which silently evaluated false (BareKit.xcframework ships no module map, same finding as flutter_pear-ovt.1.4/1.7's SPM path) and the fallback branch compiled instead -- otool -L on Runner.debug.dylib showed no BareKit reference at all. Forcing a REAL link via the bridging-header trick (#import <BareKit/BareKit.h>, no module map needed) exposed the true failure: 'fatal error: BareKit/BareKit.h file not found' even though the build log confirmed the script_phase ran and copied the file BEFORE the Swift compile step -- CocoaPods' own vendored_frameworks FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS wiring is fixed at pod-install-time xcframework-slice analysis, which can't see a file that doesn't exist yet, so a build-time-only fetch never gets a working search path regardless of script_phase ordering. REDIRECT (b) WORKING: moved the exact same copy logic into the Podfile's pre_install hook (runs during pod install, before CocoaPods analyzes vendored_frameworks/writes its xcconfig) -- clean pod install with the framework still absent from disk succeeded (marker printed pre-Xcode-project-generation), and the SAME bridging-header build now succeeds for real: otool -L on Runner.debug.dylib shows @rpath/BareKit.framework/BareKit, and the linked-in code's own string ('FEAS5: BareKit linked, config=...') is present in the binary, proving BareWorkletConfiguration.default() actually executed. CONCLUSION for the future E3 compat podspec: do NOT rely on a script_phase alone for a build-time-fetched xcframework; the fetch must happen at pod-install time (a pre_install/post_install Podfile hook, or equivalently a custom download step invoked before  proper) so CocoaPods' own search-path generation sees the real file. prepare_command remains ruled out per this task's own context (never runs for :path-installed pods, and Flutter installs all plugin pods that way). Redirect (c) (a real pod dependency carrying the framework, i.e. committing it like the SPM path already does) was not tested since (b) already answered the question with a viable, minimal-diff mechanism. All experiment files stayed in the scratchpad; repo working tree confirmed untouched (git status clean of any FEAS-5 artifacts).
