title: FEAS-3: preflight ordering — can a script phase fire before SwiftPM/CocoaPods resolution and link errors?
description:
CONTEXT: Epic flutter_pear-ovt.1 feasibility check 3 (design decision D18 depends on it). The v0.2 plan wants a build preflight (arch-slice and deployment-target checks printing flutter_pear-branded messages) in Package.swift and the compat podspec. Open question: does a script phase actually RUN BEFORE SwiftPM resolution errors or link errors reach the user? If not, the branded preflight can never fire first and the troubleshooting docs must carry that path instead (writing those docs is a later epic — this task only records the answer). Known mechanics: SwiftPM resolves binaryTargets (URL plus checksum) at dependency-resolution time, before build-tool plugins run; CocoaPods runs script_phases at build time while vendored_frameworks references resolve at pod install; prepare_command is irrelevant because CocoaPods never runs prepare_command for :path-installed pods (CocoaPods issue #2187) and Flutter installs ALL plugin pods as :path development pods. All experiment projects are throwaway — build them in the session scratchpad; commit nothing and touch no repo files.
DO:
1. SPM experiment A: scratch Package.swift with a prebuild command plugin (or preflight script target) printing a distinctive marker string, plus a deliberately broken URL binaryTarget (bad checksum or 404). Run swift build and xcodebuild; capture output; note whether the marker appears before the resolution/checksum error.
2. SPM experiment B: valid binaryTarget but an arch-mismatch link failure (a device-only slice built for the simulator). Note whether any script phase output precedes the LINK error.
3. CocoaPods experiment: scratch iOS app plus a local pod with a script_phase (execution_position before_compile) printing a marker, and a vendored_frameworks reference that is broken at pod install and at build. Run pod install and xcodebuild; note marker-vs-error ordering for each error class (install-time resolution vs build-time link).
4. bd update flutter_pear-ovt.1 --append-notes with a note starting FEAS-PREFLIGHT-ORDERING: giving per-path YES/NO (can a preflight fire before the user-visible error?) with the captured orderings; where the answer is NO, state explicitly that troubleshooting docs carry that path (D18).
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- SPM answers recorded: marker-vs-resolution-error ordering AND marker-vs-link-error ordering, each YES/NO with captured output.
- CocoaPods answers recorded: script_phase vs install-time resolution error and vs build-time link error, YES/NO with captured output.
- No repo working-tree changes (scratchpad only).
- The epic note begins FEAS-PREFLIGHT-ORDERING:.
AUTO-VALIDATION:
- The swift build, xcodebuild, and pod install transcripts (showing marker line position relative to error line) exist in the scratchpad and are summarized in the epic note.
- cd /Volumes/mandark-1Tb/projects/loabletech/flutter_pear && git status --porcelain | wc -l prints the same count before and after this task (record both).
- bd show flutter_pear-ovt.1 | grep -c FEAS-PREFLIGHT-ORDERING prints at least 1.
REVIEW BEFORE CLOSE:
1. Run every AUTO-VALIDATION command; paste a short evidence summary into this issue with bd update <this-issue-id> --append-notes (never bare --notes, it overwrites).
2. Run melos run analyze from the repo root and the test suite of every touched package (flutter test from the package dir; for pear-end changes run npm test in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end).
3. Review the implementation: read the full git diff and check it line-by-line against every ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA bullet; fix any gap before closing. If a criterion cannot be met, record why with --append-notes and flag with bd human instead of closing.
4. NEVER run git commit or git push — leave all changes uncommitted in the working tree for the developer.
5. Close with bd close <this-issue-id> --reason and a one-line summary. If you discovered follow-up work beyond this task's scope, file a NEW bd issue for it instead of expanding this one.
CONSTRAINTS: record-only feasibility — no packaging or host formalization in the repo; do not relitigate the SPM-first or script_phase-over-prepare_command decisions.
notes: AUTO-VALIDATION: swift build (expA), xcodebuild (expB), transcripts saved under the session scratchpad's feas3/ dir (build4.log for expA showing the 404+no-marker result; build.log for expb_consumer showing the marker-before-slice-error result at lines 1746 vs 1804). git status --porcelain | wc -l: 13 before this task, 13 after (unchanged -- confirmed no repo working-tree changes). bd show flutter_pear-ovt.1 | grep -c FEAS-PREFLIGHT-ORDERING -> 1 (2 total notes, both start with the marker as required).
close_reason: FEAS-PREFLIGHT-ORDERING answered with a real empirical split: SPM/CocoaPods RESOLUTION-time failures (bad checksum, 404, pod-install-time missing vendored framework) always happen before any preflight plugin/script_phase can run -- troubleshooting docs must own that path (D18). Xcode's xcframework SLICE-SELECTION / arch-mismatch failures happen AFTER build tool plugins (SPM) and script_phases (CocoaPods) already ran -- confirmed via build logs showing the plugin marker line number before the slice error's line number -- so the v0.2 plan's actual target use case (arch-slice/deployment-target preflight checks) IS achievable. Repo working tree confirmed untouched (git status count identical before/after); all experiments confined to the scratchpad.

title: v0.2 E6: docs, doctor iOS, publish 0.2.0 (T4)
description:
Docs, doctor iOS support, changelog contract, and the 0.2.0 publish (T4). Plan: ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-flutter_pear/andrewloable-main-design-20260706-031805.md (steps 6-7, Developer Experience section incl. DX scorecard, DX2 #1-#8, CEO rows on supersession + honest labeling). Runs last; depends on the example-app and tests epics being done.

CONTEXT AN IMPLEMENTER NEEDS: all three packages (flutter_pear, flutter_pear_bare, flutter_pear_test) are published on pub.dev (0.0.1 when the plan was written — check each pubspec.yaml for the current version before bumping; the v0.1 milestone name does not equal the pub version). Version policy (standing decision): ALL THREE move to 0.2.0 IN LOCKSTEP, with a 0.2.0-dev.1 prerelease first so the upgrade fixtures can run against HOSTED archives before the real release. README snippets are executable (tool/extract_snippets.dart generates test/readme_snippets.g.dart — new snippets must compile). pub.dev score target >=130 (pana). PUBLISHING IS OUTWARD-FACING: run dart pub publish only with explicit developer confirmation per package per version; NEVER git commit/push automatically (developer does that by hand).

TASKS:
1. README (flutter_pear): iOS quick start + the 5-step Enable iOS recipe for an existing v0.1 Android app (standing DX decision D-recipe); restated TTHW claims P50<=5min / P90<=10min (DX2 #1 — the old headline claims drifted from settled facts; recompute from the final recipe); download-size disclosure using the numbers measured in the tests epic (committed addon xcframeworks: accept-and-disclose, standing decision 3); received-file PATH VISIBILITY per platform (DX2: where files actually land — iOS Documents subtree visible in Files app, Android location + openability).
2. Honest platform-behavior docs: iOS background execution is foregroundOnly (or the D11 evidence outcome) — say plainly the OS suspends the swarm in background and what linger can/cannot do, so the OS killing a swarm is not blamed on the library; the release is labeled SIMULATOR-VALIDATED (standing decision 1, pinned validationTier semantics); the Local Network permission section is the TOP sim-invisible risk: works-on-sim/fails-on-device symptom table, required Info.plist key(s) from the spike's TCC answer, in-app recovery pattern.
3. Troubleshooting: BareKit fetch failures (the two-link checksum chain in barekit-pin.json — what to check when the repacked release asset or upstream is unreachable), preflight error catalogue from the host epic, CocoaPods-compat path notes incl. the D18 ordering outcome, Xcode Cloud cache-collision note (flutter/flutter#187710).
4. doctor iOS (DX2 #4): extend packages/flutter_pear/bin/doctor.dart + lib/src/doctor_report.dart with PURE-DART iOS checks (no Node required): Xcode/simulator present, deployment target sane, consumer Info.plist has NSLocalNetworkUsageDescription, barekit-pin.json URLs reachable/checksums well-formed, committed addon xcframeworks present. Follow the existing check/report structure and its tests (test/doctor_report_test.dart, tool/doctor-checks.js pattern for parity where relevant).
5. Changelog contract (standing DX decision): document the versioning/breaking-change policy (lockstep versions, what counts as breaking for the RPC schema/bundle), then write CHANGELOG.md entries for all three packages for 0.2.0.
6. Sync canonical docs: project_plan.md — replace the CocoaPods-podspec wording (lines 77/83) with SPM-first + compat podspec and mark the v0.2 milestone scope (a supersession note exists in the CEO plan: ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-flutter_pear/ceo-plans/2026-07-06-flutter-pear-v02-ios.md); COMPATIBILITY.md — add the iOS columns (plugin / Bare Kit / Hyper* / min Flutter / min iOS) keeping the no-CI header intact; CLAUDE.md Current status + toolchain rows (Xcode moves from v0.2-not-needed to needed); LICENSING.md if the iOS artifact story changed anything.
7. PUBLISH SEQUENCE (each publish gated on developer confirmation): (a) release_gate.sh green; (b) publish 0.2.0-dev.1 for all three (dependency order: flutter_pear_bare, flutter_pear, flutter_pear_test); (c) run the four upgrade fixtures from the tests epic against the HOSTED dev archives — any failure loops back before the final; (d) publish 0.2.0 all three; (e) developer tags v0.2.0 and pushes BY HAND; (f) post-publish confirmation: pub.dev renders the iOS platform badge, score holds, README renders, example quick start runs from hosted deps.

ACCEPTANCE: every doc task merged with snippets compiling via extract_snippets; doctor reports a clean iOS section on this Mac and correctly flags a missing Info.plist key in a fixture; project_plan.md/COMPATIBILITY.md/CLAUDE.md consistent with shipped reality; 0.2.0 live for all three packages with the iOS badge rendering and fixtures having passed against hosted archives.

title: v0.2 E5: tests, upgrade fixtures, release_gate.sh
description:
Tests, upgrade fixtures, and the single release-gate command. Plan: ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-flutter_pear/andrewloable-main-design-20260706-031805.md (Test Requirements / 5A batch section, eng 5A/6A, Eng2 #7, DX2 #49/#53). Test authoring can run in parallel with other epics; the gate run itself needs everything else done.

CONTEXT AN IMPLEMENTER NEEDS: this repo has NO CI (deliberate, standing decision — see COMPATIBILITY.md header); every gate is run BY HAND. The v0.1 pattern was ad-hoc manual steps; v0.2 consolidates into ONE script so nothing is forgotten. Existing infra: melos run analyze / melos run test --no-select, packages/flutter_pear/tool/fresh_machine_check.sh, tool/check_compatibility.dart, pear-end JS tests under packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/test/ (node), pana score target >=130, flutter_pear_test in-memory fake used by all Dart wrapper tests.

TASKS:
1. The 5A test batch (read the plan's Test Requirements section for the authoritative list and wording). Highlights: (a) CRITICAL pack regression test — if the spike found bare-pack deterministic, assert byte-identical rebuild; otherwise assert rebuild-matches-committed-artifacts (Eng2 #7) so a stale committed bundle/addon set cannot ship silently; if per-platform bundles were forced, the regression covers the ANDROID ASSET SET too (Eng2 #8); (b) handshake/version-mismatch coverage for the iOS path (kPearEndBundleVersion vs baked bundle version); (c) lifecycle suspend/resume tests for the D11 outcome including that native suspend honors Dart-configured linger; (d) hot-restart generation/stale-exit coverage exercised for iOS (the machinery from flutter_pear-3vh has no iOS coverage today); (e) hostile-drive mirroring test at the JS level (symlink + deep-path rejection emits the typed warning) plus a Dart-side test that the warning event surfaces — pear-end change itself lives in the pack epic.
2. flutter_pear_test fake parity: Pear.platformInfo (both fields: backgroundExecution enum + validationTier) and the drive-mirror warning event exist on the fake with the same shapes as the real path; parity asserted by test.
3. All FOUR upgrade/consumer fixtures (standing DX decision), each a minimal throwaway app OUTSIDE the melos workspace resolving packages from hosted pub (run at T4 against the hosted 0.2.0-dev.1 archives, NOT path deps): (a) fresh Android consumer, (b) fresh iOS consumer, (c) existing 0.1.x Android consumer upgrading to 0.2.0, (d) 0.1.x consumer upgrading AND enabling iOS via the documented recipe. Each fixture: pub add/upgrade succeeds, app builds, Pear.start() reaches the handshake (device/sim for its platform).
4. release_gate.sh (single command, repo root or tool/): melos bootstrap check, melos run analyze, melos run test --no-select, pear-end npm test, dart run tool/check_compatibility.dart, pana on all three packages with score threshold, license/THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES presence check, fresh_machine_check.sh, :pack regression test, iOS sim boot smoke (worklet handshake), flutter build apk for the example, flutter build ipa --no-codesign for the example + ARCHIVE INSPECTION (assert BareKit.xcframework and every addon xcframework are embedded in the .xcarchive and NSLocalNetworkUsageDescription is present in the built Info.plist — this is the no-hardware substitute for device validation, standing decision 1). Any failure = nonzero exit + a summary of which gate failed. Print a final checklist of the MANUAL steps the script cannot do (T3 recording verified, physical-Android side of T2).
5. Measure and record example-app APK/IPA size deltas versus v0.1 (eng 6A) — output stored in the epic notes and fed to the docs epic disclosure.

ACCEPTANCE: release_gate.sh runs green end-to-end on this Mac; every 5A test exists and fails when its guarded regression is introduced (spot-check by mutation, e.g. bump kPearEndBundleVersion without repacking); the four fixtures are committed with a README each and pass at T4; size numbers recorded.
notes:
5.13 size-delta results (release build mode, measured via packages/flutter_pear_example/tool/measure_size_delta.sh on 2026-07-06):
- flutter_pear v0.1 (tag v0.0.1) Android APK: 205692202 bytes (196.16 MB)
- flutter_pear v0.2 (working tree) Android APK: 205697133 bytes (196.17 MB)
- APK delta (v0.2 minus v0.1): 4931 bytes (0.00 MB) -- negligible; the Android APK build never touches ios/ so the committed xcframeworks do not inflate it. The D20 accept-and-disclose cost is a PUB PACKAGE DOWNLOAD size concern (flutter/flutter#130210), not an APK size concern -- this measurement confirms the two are independent.
- flutter_pear v0.2 iOS Runner.app (absolute, du -sk of Runner.xcarchive/Products/Applications/Runner.app; NO v0.1 iOS baseline exists, v0.1 was Android-only): 62570496 bytes (59.67 MB)
For the docs epic's 6A disclosure and D20 changelog size line.

title: v0.2 E4: example app iOS runner + hardened file-drop demo (T3 gate)
description:
Example app iOS runner + hardened cross-platform file-drop demo — this demo IS the release gate (T3). Plan: ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-flutter_pear/andrewloable-main-design-20260706-031805.md (step 5, Design Review section incl. the interaction state table and the 10 locked design decisions, DES-T1..T6, CEO row on hardened-demo gate). Depends on the Swift host epic for on-device runs; the Dart-side UI/state-machine work can start in parallel against the flutter_pear_test fake.

CONTEXT AN IMPLEMENTER NEEDS: packages/flutter_pear_example currently has Android-only runners plus hand-rolled platform channels: lib/qr_scanner_channel.dart + android QrScannerActivity.kt (QR pairing scan), lib/file_picker_channel.dart (Android file pick), lib/file_drop_screen.dart (file-drop demo), lib/pairing_screens.dart, lib/main.dart. The design review REJECTED the current demo as launch-ready: it is diagnostic tooling (protocol logs, key fragments, manual Check-for-files polling), not the AirDrop-without-Apple story. STANDING DECISION: the HARDENED demo gates the v0.2 release — do not split them. Historical Android test device: adb 192.168.0.251:5555.

TASKS:
1. iOS runner: cd packages/flutter_pear_example and flutter create --platforms=ios . — commit it. Info.plist MUST carry NSLocalNetworkUsageDescription with a real user-facing string (CEO CRITICAL: this TCC prompt is SIM-INVISIBLE — the simulator does not enforce it, real iPhones hard-fail LAN peer connections without it) plus whatever the spike's TCC feasibility answer requires (possibly NSBonjourServices / multicast entitlement).
2. iOS counterparts for the two hand-rolled channels: document picker (UIDocumentPickerViewController) behind file_picker_channel.dart, and a QR scan path behind qr_scanner_channel.dart (AVFoundation). Keep the Dart channel API unchanged so screens stay platform-neutral. Any new dependency must be MIT/Apache-2.0/BSD/ISC.
3. Demo hardening per the 10 locked design decisions (Design Review section of the plan): (a) home flow is Send file / Receive file — not six test routes; (b) full transfer state machine with exact user-facing copy per the plan's interaction state table (waiting-for-peer vs ready, selected vs sending, byte progress, interrupted+retry, duplicate filename, empty receive state, lost-connection); (c) replace the developer log with a file card: filename, size, progress, peer/device label, result, retry/open/share actions; (d) receiving is AUTOMATIC — the Check-for-files button must disappear; (e) received-file destination + success affordances: Open / Share / Show in Files (iOS: the Documents subtree from the host epic + UIDocumentInteractionController or share sheet; Android per DES-T1: received file must be OPENABLE — FileProvider content URI + ACTION_VIEW, not a bare path log line); (f) invite copy/share affordance — no raw base64 wall as primary UI; (g) Local Network permission DENIED recovery inside the flow: detect, banner, Settings deep-link (a doc note does not rescue an end user); (h) reconnecting banner on connection loss.
4. Responsive + accessibility floor from the design review: scrollable home, QR size adapts, long codes wrap, semantic labels on state changes (announced, not color-only), large-text pass. Keep it the demo-app floor, not a design-system project.
5. T3 GATE EXECUTION: cross-platform file drop BOTH directions (iOS sim sends via document picker, physical Android receives and opens it; Android sends, iOS receives and opens it), recorded per the launch-video storyboard in the plan's design section (which device initiates, what each screen shows, final proof the file opens).
6. Widget tests for the new screens/state machine against the flutter_pear_test fake (existing patterns: test/pairing_screens_test.dart, test/demo_home_screen_test.dart), including semantics assertions.

ACCEPTANCE: T3 passes with the recording captured; no manual polling anywhere; a received file is openable on BOTH platforms via the in-app affordance; Local Network denial path demonstrated (real device or code-path test) with in-flow recovery; widget tests green.

title: E9.4 follow-up: real per-package integration tests (flutter_pear_bare lifecycle + E5 wrappers)
description:
CONTEXT: E9.4 (flutter_pear-df9.4) closing review confirmed that today only flutter_pear_example has a real integration_test/ suite (ipc_transport_test.dart) running against the real Bare Kit worklet on-device. STEP 2 of df9.4 explicitly names flutter_pear_bare lifecycle (suspend/resume, hot-restart reattach-or-kill) and each E5 wrapper (PearBee, PearDrive, PearPairing, PearBase) as needing their own per-package integration coverage against the real worklet, not just the flutter_pear_test in-memory fake. None of flutter_pear_bare, flutter_pear, or flutter_pear_test currently have an integration_test/ directory at all.

STEPS:
1. Add a hydrated example app (flutter create --platforms=android .) under flutter_pear_bare/example if one doesn't already run, with its own integration_test/ target.
2. Add a real-device/emulator integration test exercising flutter_pear_bare's BareWorklet lifecycle: suspend/resume tied to AppLifecycleState, and hot-restart reattach-or-kill (E6) against a REAL worklet process, not the fake.
3. For each E5 wrapper (PearBee, PearDrive, PearPairing, PearBase) that doesn't yet have a real-worklet integration test, add one under its own package's example/integration_test (or flutter_pear's, if that's where the wrapper API lives) -- see flutter_pear-df9.4's STEP 2 note for the desktop CLI peer (E7.3) as the scriptable second peer where a second peer is needed.
4. ci.yml's emulator-x86_64/emulator-arm64 jobs already loop over every packages/*/integration_test (and packages/*/example/integration_test) directory found and run it -- no ci.yml changes should be needed once these suites exist, only verify the loop actually picks them up.

VALIDATION: each new suite runs green in the real emulator-x86_64 and emulator-arm64 CI jobs (not just locally); flutter_pear-df9.4's own ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA ("run per-package integration tests") is then literally true, not just infrastructure-ready.
acceptance_criteria: Each new integration_test suite runs green on a real local Android emulator (adb-attached, e.g. emulator-5554) when run BY HAND -- "real CI" is not a criterion since this repo has no CI. Evidence: command + pass output for each suite in the closing comment.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED AND LOCALLY VALIDATED (this session): all 5 wrappers this ticket named (PearBee, PearDrive, PearPairing, PearBase) PLUS PearStore (a gap this ticket itself missed but flutter_pear-doi 2vz.2 needed -- added to close it) now have real on-device integration_test suites in packages/flutter_pear_example/integration_test/: bee_transport_test.dart, drive_transport_test.dart, pairing_transport_test.dart, base_transport_test.dart, store_transport_test.dart. Each connects the REAL Dart wrapper API on a REAL emulator (emulator-5554) to the matching NEW desktop CLI peer harness (tool/peer.js --bee/--drive/--base/--store, tool/pairing-peer.js), all already independently proven process-to-process this session. Every suite was run for real, multiple times each (including my own independent re-runs, not just trusting subagent reports), with genuine cross-runtime (phone Dart <-> desktop Node) proof each time -- keys/values/handshakes cross-checked from BOTH sides logs, not just the phone side.

flutter_pear_bare/example/ was hydrated for real (flutter create --platforms=android, previously just a placeholder README) with integration_test/lifecycle_test.dart covering BareWorklet suspend/resume (E6.1) plus an honest, non-fabricated characterization of the reattach-or-kill (E6.3) native decision logic -- the raw MethodChannel is driven directly to prove the ACTUAL native reattach-vs-kill code path a real hot restart depends on, since a literal Dart isolate teardown cannot be triggered by a test against itself; flagged as a real residual gap needing a manual runbook step, not fabricated as fully covered.

Real bugs found and fixed along the way: pairing_transport_test.dart hit a genuine race on its FIRST real-device run (Method.CONNECTION_WRITE transiently returns UNKNOWN_PEER even moments after SWARM_CONNECTION fires, due to real connection churn) -- fixed in tool/pairing-peer.js by treating it like a missed ack and resending, reproduced clean on 2 subsequent real runs after the fix; tool/pairing-peer.js also had a mandatory --bootstrap flag that made it structurally unable to ever talk to a real phone (which cannot join a private testnet) -- made optional. store_transport_test.dart surfaced a real Method.CORE_GET semantics nuance (throws immediately at/past current length rather than blocking, unlike a raw Hypercore) requiring an explicit wait-for-length helper instead of a naive read loop. Also found and documented (not fixed, out of scope): BareWorklet.start(bundlePath:) is a dead parameter on Android -- native never reads it, so the documented custom-worklet advanced path is non-functional today.

REAL CI-WIRING GAP FOUND AND FIXED (by me, reviewing the subagent work): ci.yml own per-package integration-test discovery loop (added during df9.4 own fix pass) explicitly skipped the ENTIRE flutter_pear_example package to avoid double-running ipc_transport_test.dart -- meaning it would have silently NEVER run any of the 6 new suites in CI, since they all live in that same package own integration_test/ directory. Rewrote both emulator-x86_64 and emulator-arm64 jobs loops to run each non-benchmark integration_test file individually across every package uniformly (no more package-level skip), verified via a local dry-run simulation of the exact loop logic that it now correctly discovers and would run all 7 real suites (6 in flutter_pear_example + the new flutter_pear_bare one) while still correctly excluding bulk_transport_benchmark_test.dart. YAML/bash syntax re-validated after the edit; melos run analyze/test both still green.

NOT CLOSING per this ticket own VALIDATION line ("runs green in the real emulator-x86_64 and emulator-arm64 CI jobs (not just locally)"): no real GitHub Actions execution is possible in this dev environment (matching flutter_pear-df9.3 own honest gap for the identical reason). Every suite has been proven correct and reproducible on a real local Android emulator, multiple times, with genuine cross-runtime validation -- but literal "green in real CI" remains unverified pending an actual push. Leaving IN_PROGRESS, not closed, consistent with how df9.3 was left for the same class of gap.
FRESH INDEPENDENT RE-VERIFICATION (2026-07-05), all 6 suites, real emulator, real desktop peers, freshly randomized topics (not reruns of stale results): bee_transport_test.dart PASS, drive_transport_test.dart PASS, pairing_transport_test.dart PASS, base_transport_test.dart PASS, store_transport_test.dart PASS, ipc_transport_test.dart PASS (no desktop peer needed). Each run independently confirmed the same-session unrelated changes (GitHub Actions workflow removal, doc edits, the SWARM_JOIN hot-restart fix in pear-end/index.js) caused no regression. Full command output + peer-side cross-checks recorded per suite. This ticket own re-scoped ACCEPTANCE (manual verification, no CI required) is now fully satisfied with fresh evidence -- closing.
close_reason: All 6 real-device integration suites independently re-verified passing today with fresh evidence (see notes) -- re-scoped acceptance (manual verification, this repo has no CI) fully met.

title: flutter_pear_example's ML Kit barcode-scanning dep is proprietary (ML Kit ToS), not Apache-2.0 as its build.gradle.kts comment claimed
description: E7.2 code review (native-lifecycle/security-licensing pass) found build.gradle.kts:51 falsely claimed 'Both dependency families below are Apache-2.0'. com.google.mlkit:barcode-scanning:17.3.0's own POM declares 'ML Kit Terms of Service' (a proprietary Google EULA), and it transitively pulls in closed-source com.google.android.gms:play-services-mlkit-barcode-scanning + play-services-basement. This violates LICENSING.md's permissive-only (MIT/Apache-2.0/BSD/ISC/0BSD) rule. Comment corrected to state this accurately and flag it, but the dependency itself is unchanged -- no drop-in permissively-licensed on-device QR decoder was substituted (would be a real engineering decision: evaluate zxing-android-embedded or similar, verify it doesn't hit the same AGP9/Kotlin toolchain plugin breakage documented in flutter_pear-jqe). Needs a maintainer decision: swap the dependency, or accept + formally document the exception (this app module isn't part of the published flutter_pear package LICENSING.md strictly governs, but it's still an undisclosed proprietary chain in an open-source demo).
notes:
RESOLVED: swapped com.google.mlkit:barcode-scanning for com.google.zxing:core:3.5.3 (Apache-2.0, confirmed via its zxing-parent POM's <licenses> block; a plain JVM library with no Android Gradle module of its own, so unlike file_picker/mobile_scanner/permission_handler it carries zero AGP9/Kotlin-plugin risk -- no maintainer tradeoff needed, this is a strict improvement).

WHAT CHANGED
- android/app/build.gradle.kts: dependency swap + corrected licensing comment (was "unresolved flag", now documents the fix and why ZXing is toolchain-safe). Added testImplementation("junit:junit:4.13.2") -- test-scoped only, never bundled, so LICENSING.md's shipped-dependency scope doesn't apply regardless of its EPL-1.0 license.
- QrScannerActivity.kt: replaced ML Kit's async BarcodeScanner.process(InputImage...) with a synchronous ZXing pipeline -- extractYPlaneBytes (strips YUV_420_888 row-stride padding) -> optional rotateYPlane90 (0-3x, matching CameraX's reported rotationDegrees) -> PlanarYUVLuminanceSource -> BinaryBitmap/HybridBinarizer -> MultiFormatReader scoped to BarcodeFormat.QR_CODE with TRY_HARDER (classical decoders are less blur/skew-tolerant than ML Kit's ML-based one; this closes most of that gap). Decoding now runs on a dedicated cameraExecutor (single-thread), not the main executor -- ZXing's decode() is synchronous real CPU work, unlike ML Kit's Task-based process(); handleResult() dispatches its setResult()/finish() back via runOnUiThread since those are Activity calls. cameraExecutor shuts down in onDestroy().
- New: android/app/src/test/kotlin/.../QrScannerActivityTest.kt -- 5 JVM unit tests (no Android framework needed) using ZXing's own QRCodeWriter to encode a real QR code and feed it back through decodeQrFromYPlane, covering: unrotated decode, 90/270-degree rotation handling, null-not-exception on a blank frame, and rotateYPlane90 applied 4x returning the identity.

CODE REVIEW (medium effort): found and fixed 3 real issues before closing -- (1) decode was running synchronously on the main-thread executor (jank/ANR risk), moved to a dedicated background executor + runOnUiThread for the Activity-result calls; (2) extractYPlaneBytes's row copy used `minOf(rowStride, buffer.remaining())`, which could silently blend stale bytes from a previous row into a reused scratch buffer if a read ever came up short -- changed to always read the full rowStride, so a genuine shortfall throws (caught by the existing catch-all as "skip this frame") instead of corrupting data silently; (3) added TRY_HARDER hint for real-world scan robustness. Two other findings (no onDestroy() scanner cleanup; broad catch-all hiding decode bugs) were confirmed non-issues/unchanged-from-original-ML-Kit-behavior respectively.

VALIDATION: melos analyze: 0 issues, all packages. ./gradlew :app:testDebugUnitTest: 5/5 new tests pass (verified via the JUnit XML report, not just "build succeeded"). flutter build apk --debug: succeeds. Live-tested on an emulator twice (before and after the review fixes): QrScannerActivity launches, binds a real CameraX preview without crashing, no FATAL/Exception in logcat, preview renders smoothly (background executor doesn't stutter it). Could NOT get a real QR code to appear in the emulator's virtual-scene camera framing (tried -virtualscene-poster wall=/table=; the poster surfaces aren't in the default camera framing and panning them headlessly needs sensor injection this session didn't chase down) -- so the actual camera-frame decode path (extractYPlaneBytes against REAL sensor data, not synthetic ZXing-encoded bytes) is unit-tested and structurally unchanged-in-risk from the CameraX plumbing E7.2 already live-validated, but not itself live-decode-tested end to end. This matches the same class of gap already accepted project-wide for physical-hardware legs (see flutter_pear-doi) -- not a new corner cut for this ticket specifically, since a full live QR-scan-to-pairing round trip was never achieved in any prior session either (E7.2's own closing notes).
close_reason: Swapped ML Kit for ZXing (Apache-2.0, zero AGP9/Kotlin-plugin risk being a pure-JVM lib) -- a strict improvement, no tradeoff needed. 5 new JVM unit tests directly verify the decode algorithm (including rotation handling) against real ZXing-encoded QR data. Code-reviewed and fixed 3 issues (main-thread blocking, silent stale-byte blend risk, added TRY_HARDER for real-world robustness). Live-tested: launches, binds camera, no crash. Full camera-frame decode not live-tested (emulator virtual-scene poster framing limitation) -- same class of hardware-validation gap already accepted project-wide.

title: file_picker 11.0.2 Android build fails under this project's AGP 9.0.1/Kotlin 2.3.20: 'cannot find symbol: class FilePickerPlugin'
description:
CONTEXT: found while building E7.7's file-drop demo screen (packages/flutter_pear_example/lib/file_drop_screen.dart), which needs a file picker to let the user choose a file to send via PearDrive.put(). Added file_picker as a new flutter_pear_example dependency (MIT licensed, matches LICENSING.md constraints).
SYMPTOM: 'flutter build apk --debug' fails at :app:compileDebugJavaWithJavac with 'cannot find symbol: class FilePickerPlugin, location: package com.mr.flutter.plugin.filepicker' in the generated GeneratedPluginRegistrant.java. Confirmed NOT a stale-cache issue: reproduced after flutter clean, gradlew --stop, and manually deleting .gradle/app-build/build directories.
ROOT CAUSE (partial): file_picker 11.0.2's android/build.gradle has 'def isAgp9OrAbove = ...; if (!isAgp9OrAbove) { apply plugin: org.jetbrains.kotlin.android }' -- it deliberately skips applying the Kotlin Gradle Plugin under AGP 9+ to rely on AGP 9's built-in Kotlin support instead. This project pins AGP 9.0.1 (packages/flutter_pear_example/android/settings.gradle.kts) + Kotlin 2.3.20. Something about that combination leaves the plugin's compiled Kotlin classes (FilePickerPlugin.kt et al) invisible to the Java-side GeneratedPluginRegistrant at compile time -- full root cause not yet isolated (didn't dig into AGP9's built-in-Kotlin source-set wiring specifics).
ALSO TRIED: downgrading to file_picker 8.1.7 -- different failure (its own build.gradle hardcodes compileSdk 34, which conflicts with flutter_plugin_android_lifecycle's transitive requirement of compileSdk 36+ pulled in by this Flutter version's plugin ecosystem). No file_picker version currently builds cleanly against this project's AGP9/Kotlin2.3.20/Flutter-3.44.4 combination.
IMPACT: file_drop_screen.dart is code-complete, analyze-clean, and unit/widget-tested, but CANNOT be live-device-validated (file transfer, 100MB no-OOM check, byte-hash verification) until this Android build issue is resolved -- blocking E7.7's full closure.
acceptance_criteria: flutter build apk succeeds with file_picker as a dependency under this project's AGP/Kotlin/Flutter versions -- either a working file_picker version/config is found, this project's AGP pin is adjusted to something broadly compatible, or a documented workaround (e.g. a build.gradle override) is applied and explained.
notes:
Not a regression in anything this session touched -- file_picker was newly added for E7.7. This project's AGP 9.0.1/Kotlin 2.3.20 pins are very recent/bleeding-edge; worth checking whether other native plugins added in the future hit the same class of issue, which would suggest downgrading AGP is the more durable fix versus chasing per-plugin compatibility.
ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED (via a live experiment, then reverted):

Flipping packages/flutter_pear_example/android/gradle.properties' android.builtInKotlin from false to true (matching what file_picker's own android/build.gradle assumes for AGP9+ -- its isAgp9OrAbove check skips applying org.jetbrains.kotlin.android believing the project is on built-in Kotlin) does NOT fix file_picker. Instead it immediately breaks flutter_pear_bare (this project's own native plugin) with:

  "The 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.android' plugin is no longer required for Kotlin support since AGP 9.0. Solution: Remove the 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.android' plugin from this project's build file: ../../flutter_pear_bare/android/build.gradle."
  followed by: "project ':flutter_pear_bare' does not specify compileSdk"

flutter_pear_bare's own android/build.gradle still explicitly applies org.jetbrains.kotlin.android (has NOT migrated to AGP9's built-in Kotlin) -- this is why the project pins android.builtInKotlin=false in the first place. But that same false setting is exactly what leaves file_picker (whose build.gradle assumes AGP9 == built-in Kotlin, unconditionally, with no escape hatch of its own) never applying its OWN Kotlin plugin, so its Kotlin source never compiles -- hence "cannot find symbol: FilePickerPlugin".

This is a genuine, structural, PROJECT-WIDE incompatibility, not something fixable per-plugin: flutter_pear_bare requires builtInKotlin=false; file_picker (and, per research done for E7.2's QR-scanner needs, mobile_scanner AND permission_handler -- both have their own OPEN, unresolved upstream issues about this exact AGP9 gap) require builtInKotlin=true (or at least behave as if it's true). Both cannot be satisfied by one project-wide flag simultaneously today.

Confirmed reverted cleanly: android.builtInKotlin is back to false, file_picker dependency restored, build is back to its prior (file_picker-only-broken, everything-else-fine) state.

The only ways forward for ANY new native Android plugin in this project, right now:
1. Migrate flutter_pear_bare itself off org.jetbrains.kotlin.android to AGP9 built-in Kotlin (a real, separate migration task on the core plugin -- unblocks the whole plugin ecosystem project-wide, but is its own project).
2. Hand-roll any needed native functionality as Kotlin code owned directly by the example app's own android/app module (not a separate Gradle subproject/plugin) -- sidesteps the issue entirely since the app module's own Kotlin compiles fine today, but is real native-code effort per feature.
3. Wait for the upstream ecosystem (file_picker/mobile_scanner/permission_handler) to ship AGP9-compatible releases.
close_reason: Resolved via hand-rolled native Storage Access Framework picker (documented workaround per this issue's own ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA). Re-confirmed fresh this session: flutter build apk --debug succeeded 3 separate times while implementing/fixing flutter_pear-u8y.7, and the picker was live-exercised on a real emulator to pick and send a real 5MB file successfully.

title: blind-pairing real invite/accept round trip fails: MemberRequest.open() rejects with 'Failed to open invite with provided key'
description:
CONTEXT: found while building E7.3's desktop CLI peer (flutter_pear_example/tool/peer.js), which needed a --create-invite/--invite mode mirroring pear-end/index.js's PAIRING_CREATE_INVITE/PAIRING_ACCEPT_INVITE handlers exactly (same BlindPairing calls, same key usage). A live two-process round trip (one process as inviter via BlindPairing.createInvite+pairing.addMember, another as acceptor via pairing.addCandidate) never completed successfully, even after fixing two real bugs along the way in my own code:
1. request.open() needs createInvite's returned publicKey, not the raw resourceKey seed (fixed).
2. addCandidate's userData must be an explicit Buffer (Buffer.alloc(0)), not undefined (fixed).
After both fixes, the member side's onadd still throws 'Failed to open invite with provided key' from blind-pairing-core's MemberRequest.open() -- reproduced not just over the real network but in a SAME-PROCESS, NO-NETWORK isolated repro (constructing a CandidateRequest and MemberRequest directly against each other with matching keys), ruling out network/timing flakiness as the cause. Isolated crypto-only check (createInvite -> decodeInvite -> re-derive keyPair from seed) DOES round-trip correctly, so the raw key derivation itself is not the problem -- the bug is somewhere in how CandidateRequest's actual auth payload construction or MemberRequest's decrypt/verify interacts with the installed blind-pairing/blind-pairing-core versions.
IMPORTANT: pear-end/index.js's own PAIRING_CREATE_INVITE/PAIRING_ACCEPT_INVITE handlers use this exact same pattern and, as far as I can tell from the test suite, have ONLY ever been exercised against flutter_pear_test's in-memory fake, never a real end-to-end blind-pairing invite exchange -- so this may be a live, previously-undiscovered bug in PearPairing's real (non-fake) code path, not just an issue in my new example-app code.
FILES: packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/index.js (PAIRING_CREATE_INVITE/PAIRING_ACCEPT_INVITE), packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/node_modules/blind-pairing, packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/node_modules/blind-pairing-core, packages/flutter_pear/lib/src/pairing.dart (PearPairing.createInvite/acceptInvite -- the Dart-side surface that would be affected).
REPRO: packages/flutter_pear_example/tool/peer.js's git history (E7.3 commit) has the removed --create-invite/--invite implementation showing the exact BlindPairing calls used; can be resurrected from git log for a starting point.
acceptance_criteria: Either: a real two-process (or worklet-vs-worklet) invite create+accept round trip succeeds and PearPairing gets a test proving it against something other than the in-memory fake, OR the root cause is identified as an installed-dependency version mismatch/misuse and fixed with a regression test.
notes: Not blocking E7.3's close -- topic mode (the epic's other supported join method) works reliably and is what shipped; the phone side (E7.2) doesn't have invite-creation UI yet either, so nothing regresses. But this is a real correctness gap in PearPairing's REAL (non-fake) path that should be resolved before E7.2/QR-pairing ships anything relying on real invites working end-to-end.
close_reason: Two distinct root causes found+fixed (userData null-collapse; confirm-key discoveryKey mismatch), real (non-fake) regression test added, second-model reviewed (2 issues found+fixed), CI flakiness from a 3rd unrelated pre-existing test also fixed

comments.author: Andrew Loable
comments.text:
Root cause found and fixed (two distinct bugs, both entirely within packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/index.js -- no node_modules changes), verified with a new real (non-fake) regression test.

BUG 1 (this ticket's own title): blind-pairing-core@2.10.1's compact-encoding `optionalBuffer` codec decodes a zero-length Buffer back as `null` on the wire. `verifyReceipt` returns exactly that decoded userData as its SUCCESS value, so when a candidate's userData is empty (the common case -- PearPairing.acceptInvite's userData is optional and rarely supplied), verifyReceipt returns `null`, which its caller `openAuth` misreads via `if (!verifyReceipt(...))` as failure, throwing 'Invalid reply' -- rewrapped by MemberRequest.open()'s catch-all into the reported "Failed to open invite with provided key". Proven conclusively (not just inferred): manually reimplemented decrypt succeeded independently; intercepted sodium.crypto_sign_verify_detached directly and confirmed it returns `true` for the exact call that "fails" -- the signature is genuinely valid, only the null-vs-empty-buffer collapse trips the caller.

Fix: encodeOptionalBufferSafe/decodeOptionalBufferSafe prefix a 1-byte flag onto every optional-buffer byte string crossing into blind-pairing (userData AND, per the review below, the confirm key), so a truly zero-length buffer never reaches the buggy path.

BUG 2 (found while chasing bug 1, more severe): blind-pairing-core's MemberRequest.confirm({key}) requires crypto.discoveryKey(key) to equal the invite's own discoveryKey -- a real protocol requirement, confirmed empirically (confirming with an unrelated random key silently times out; confirming with the invite's own internal resourceKey succeeds immediately, both traced over a real local hyperdht testnet). But PearPairingCandidate.confirm(PearKey key)'s documented contract -- and what flutter_pear_test's fake actually implements, confirmed by reading it -- lets the app pass an ARBITRARY key unrelated to the invite's internals. Before this fix, pear-end/index.js forwarded the app's key straight through as blind-pairing-core's required `key` field, which could never satisfy the discoveryKey check for ANY real app usage -- meaning no real (non-fake) pairing could EVER complete, independent of bug 1. This is why the ticket's own notes suspected the real path had never actually been exercised.

Fix: PAIRING_CREATE_INVITE now stores resourceKey + seed per invite. PAIRING_CONFIRM_CANDIDATE confirms blind-pairing-core's required `key` with the stored resourceKey (satisfies its internal check) and carries the app's actual key via blind-pairing-core's `additional` field, signed with the invite's own seed-derived keypair (crypto.sign, matching what blind-pairing-core's _openResponse verifies additional data with on the candidate's side). PAIRING_ACCEPT_INVITE now returns paired.data (the verified app key) instead of paired.key (now just the meaningless internal resourceKey). The wire contract is unchanged -- PAIRING_CONFIRM_CANDIDATE still takes {key}, PAIRING_ACCEPT_INVITE still returns {key} -- so schema.dart/pairing.dart/the fake are untouched.

New test: packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/test/pairing-real-roundtrip.test.js drives the REAL PAIRING_CREATE_INVITE/PAIRING_ACCEPT_INVITE/PAIRING_CONFIRM_CANDIDATE handlers end to end over a real, local, offline hyperdht testnet (three cases: no userData, non-empty userData, a genuine single zero-byte userData). Confirmed the test genuinely requires BOTH fixes by re-running it against a bug-2-only-reverted index.js -- all three cases failed with PAIRING_TIMEOUT.

Also discovered and fixed: blind-pairing can legitimately deliver the same candidate's request via two independent channels (DHT-poll discovery and a live swarm connection) when both peers are simultaneously reachable -- onadd fires twice for the identical token/session. This is real, pre-existing, unrelated to either bug above (reproduced across poll intervals 300ms-3000ms and testnet sizes 3-10 nodes). The new test's confirm-loop handles it robustly (confirms every notification with the same key rather than assuming exactly one). Filed as its own follow-up: flutter_pear-0zq (the app-facing impact -- a real UI could see two PAIRING_CANDIDATE events for one physical device -- needs its own de-dup fix at the pear-end layer, out of scope here).

Review: an independent second-model review, which independently re-derived and empirically verified both bugs and the fix (including re-running the test against reverted variants), found two real issues, both fixed:
1. additional.data (the app's confirm key) crossed the SAME buggy optionalBuffer path as userData but wasn't wrapped -- a malformed/empty confirm key (bypassing PearKey's debug-only length assert in a release build) would hit the same failure class at a different call site. Fixed: encodeOptionalBufferSafe/decodeOptionalBufferSafe (renamed from the userData-specific names) now wrap both userData and the confirm key consistently.
2. The test's own assertion comment incorrectly claimed the pre-fix bug surfaced as "Failed to open invite with provided key" on the acceptor -- it doesn't; both bugs fail silently on the inviter/candidate side internally and only ever reach the acceptor as PAIRING_TIMEOUT after the full bound elapses. Comment corrected to describe the real failure path.

Also fixed: package.json's test script now runs `node --test --test-concurrency=1`. The new test file's real DHT/network load was confirmed (by toggling concurrency on/off repeatedly) to occasionally flip an unrelated, pre-existing timing-sensitive autobase-recipes.test.js assertion (a crdtMap convergence check) from pass to fail under Node's default concurrent-file execution; that test passes reliably alone or under concurrency=1. Total suite runtime difference is negligible (~33s either way) -- this buys deterministic CI.

Test evidence: `npm test` in pear-end/ -- 24/24 passing, confirmed deterministic across 4 consecutive full-suite runs (0 flakes) after the concurrency fix; `melos run analyze` (zero issues, all 4 packages); `melos run test --no-select` (full Dart suite green).

title: Harden E5.2/E5.3's STORE_GET/BEE_OPEN: closed session leaked on a redundant reopen while already registered
description:
CONTEXT: found during E5.5's (flutter_pear-2vz.5) independent review, which caught the SAME pattern in DRIVE_OPEN and fixed it there (see that ticket's closing notes). The reviewer explicitly noted STORE_GET (pear-end/index.js) and BEE_OPEN share a lesser version of the same leak: `core = store.get({name: p.name})` (or `store.get(Buffer.from(p.key,'hex'))`) always constructs a FRESH Hypercore session object, every single call -- if the resulting key is already registered in `cores`/`bees` and not closed, that freshly-fetched session is silently discarded with no `.close()` call, leaking one Hypercore session per redundant re-open call for the life of the worklet.

Concrete trigger: an app calling `pear.store.get(name: 'x')` (or `pear.bee(name: 'x')`) more than once while the first handle is still open (a legitimate, documented-as-idempotent usage pattern -- "get() by name is idempotent" is literally one of this repo's own test names) leaks one Hypercore session per extra call.

Unlike DRIVE_OPEN's version of this bug (fixed in E5.5 by deferring the heavier Hyperdrive construction inside the registration guard), STORE_GET/BEE_OPEN's OWN core-fetch is unavoidably the first thing that has to happen (there's no separate "scratch" object to defer) -- so the fix here is different: either (a) close the freshly-fetched core/bee's underlying core when discarding it on the already-registered path, or (b) check the registry BEFORE calling store.get() at all if there's a way to compute the deterministic key without opening a session (verify whether corestore exposes a synchronous name->key derivation that doesn't require a full session).

STEPS: read pear-end/index.js's current STORE_GET and BEE_OPEN cases; read node_modules/corestore for session/refcounting behavior to pick the safest fix; apply it to both; add a regression test (open the same name twice while the first is still open, e.g. via a fake-level introspection hook analogous to activeBeeWatchCount, or a JS-level session-count assertion if flutter_pear-pcg's JS test harness exists by then).
notes: CLOSED: fixed in packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/index.js -- STORE_GET and BEE_OPEN now close the freshly-fetched Corestore session on the redundant-reopen path (already-registered && not closed) instead of discarding it silently, mirroring DRIVE_OPEN's existing pattern. Regression test: packages/flutter_pear/pear-end/test/index.test.js's STORE_GET/BEE_OPEN test -- monkeypatches Corestore.prototype.get to capture every session object index.js fetches, calls STORE_GET/BEE_OPEN twice with the same name while the first stays open, and asserts the registered session stays open while the redundant one's .closed becomes true. Verified this test fails without the fix (reverted index.js, reran -- 'the redundant reopen's fresh session must be closed, not leaked': false !== true) and passes with it. Test evidence: 'node --test' from packages/flutter_pear/pear-end -- 21/21 pass (18 pre-existing autobase-recipes tests + 3 new, including this one and flutter_pear-pcg's two). dart analyze packages/flutter_pear: no issues. No Dart changes needed (JS-only bug).
close_reason: STORE_GET/BEE_OPEN session leak fixed with a real JS-level regression test (see notes).

title: Harden worklet-generation handoff against stale platform-channel messages
description: CONTEXT: found during E2.6 (flutter_pear-7be.6) review. flutter_pear_bare's _ipc and _control (bare_worklet.dart) are STATIC MethodChannel/BasicMessageChannel fields shared across all BareWorklet Dart instances; each generation transition (Pear.start's version-mismatch kill+restart, or a hot-restart reattach) reassigns the handler via setMessageHandler/setMethodCallHandler rather than using a per-generation channel. Flutter's engine buffers a channel message that arrives with no handler currently registered, replaying it to whichever handler is registered next -- so a stale worklet generation's message (e.g. a delayed workletCrash self-report, or in principle any IPC data frame) that is still in flight when Dart swaps in a new BareWorklet's handler can be delivered to the NEW generation's handler instead of being dropped. PearRpc._onFrame's narrow pre-session workletCrash exemption (added in E2.6) knowingly accepts this residual risk rather than closing it -- see its doc comment in rpc.dart. The identical risk class already exists, unaddressed, in the native onCrash backstop's control channel (bare_worklet.dart's _onControlCall / FlutterPearBarePlugin.kt's reportUnexpectedExit), independent of anything E2.6 added.
acceptance_criteria: Either (a) a documented argument for why this residual risk is acceptable long-term (not just as an E2.6-scoped trade-off), or (b) a generation-tagging or channel-draining mechanism (e.g. stamp/verify a generation id at the platform-channel layer itself, not just in the JSON envelope) that closes the gap for both the IPC data channel and the control channel. Add a regression test that proves a stale generation's message can no longer be misattributed to a new one.
notes:
Not blocking E2.6's close -- the failure mode (a false WORKLET_CRASHED report) is loud and recoverable, not silent or corrupting, and requires two independent low-probability events (a real crash of the old worklet racing an unrelated restart). Deferred as a deliberate scope cut, not an oversight.
CLOSED with a hybrid resolution: closed the control-channel gap for real (option b), and strengthened the IPC-data-channel's existing narrow exemption to a permanent documented-accept (option a) rather than leaving it as a temporary E2.6-scoped trade-off.

CONTROL CHANNEL (closed): added a native-assigned generation id, tagging the platform-channel layer itself (not just the JSON envelope), per the ticket's own suggested mechanism.
- FlutterPearBarePlugin.kt: companion object gets workletGeneration (Int), bumped only on a fresh boot (never on reattach -- same native worklet, new Dart isolate). 'start' now returns {reattached, generationId}. relayFromWorklet() captures activeGeneration alongside activeIpc when it arms the read loop; reportUnexpectedExit(reason, generation) stamps it on the onWorkletExit call.
- bare_worklet.dart: BareWorklet._generationId is set from start()'s result. _onControlCall now drops an onWorkletExit call whose generationId doesn't match ours (once we know our own id) -- silently, same as any other stale-generation message. Backward compatible: if either side omits generationId (older native, or a test/fake), the check degrades to a no-op (pre-fix behavior), so nothing existing regresses.
- Regression test (bare_worklet_test.dart): boots generation 1, terminates it, boots generation 2, then delivers a stale generationId:1 onWorkletExit call to generation 2's now-current handler -- asserts generation 2 stays 'running' with zero crash events, then confirms a genuine generationId:2 exit still works. Verified this test fails without the fix (reverted bare_worklet.dart, reran -- 'a stale generation's exit report must not stop a newer, healthy generation': Expected running, Actual stopped) and passes with it. Added a second test confirming the no-generationId-at-all backward-compat path.

IPC DATA CHANNEL (accepted, documented -- not closed): the pre-session workletCrash exemption in rpc.dart (_onFrame) still has the same narrow residual risk, upgraded to a permanent rationale rather than deferring to a follow-up ticket (this WAS that follow-up). NOT closed via generation-tagging because doing so would mean tagging the platform-channel layer for EVERY IPC data-channel byte (the hot path for all worklet traffic), not just the rare crash/exit signal the control channel carries -- a much larger, riskier wire-format change for a narrower payoff. Accepted permanently because: (1) failure mode is loud (WORKLET_CRASHED) and recoverable, never silent/corrupting; (2) requires two independently unlikely events to coincide; (3) the only alternative (dropping the pre-session exemption) reintroduces the strictly worse problem E2.6 built it to prevent.

Test evidence: melos run analyze -- 4/4 packages clean. melos run test --no-select -- flutter_pear 167/167, flutter_pear_bare 16/16 (incl. 2 new), flutter_pear_test 13/13, all green. Kotlin change could NOT be compiled/run in this sandbox (no gradle/kotlinc available) -- verified by careful reading + mirroring existing patterns in the file exactly (same style as the reattached/workletIpc handling already there); real-device verification of this path is out of scope for this ticket (native worklet boot/crash paths are covered by flutter_pear-doi's final hardware pass, not per-ticket builds).
close_reason: Control-channel generation-tagging fix (native+Dart) closes that gap with a verified regression test; IPC data-channel's narrower residual risk upgraded to a permanent documented accept.

title: Bare Kit worklet fails to boot on BYD head unit: dlopen libnativehelper.so not found
description:
libbare-kit.so (bare-kit v2.3.0's own prebuilt binary, the latest release as of 2026-07-03 per upstream's releases page) directly declares NEEDED libnativehelper.so (confirmed via llvm-readelf -d against the extracted APK's lib/arm64-v8a/libbare-kit.so). libnativehelper.so is a private Android platform library; since API 24 the dynamic linker blocks third-party apps from resolving non-NDK platform-private libraries via any path (System.loadLibrary, DT_NEEDED, or direct dlopen). On the BYD AUTO head unit (Android 10 / API 29, automotive OEM ROM) this hard-fails Worklet.start() with PlatformException(worklet_start_failed, 'dlopen failed: library "libnativehelper.so" not found'). Reproduced on 2026-07-03 once flutter_pear-dpl's storage blocker was cleared and a real install was finally possible.

Critically: the SAME app (same debug APK, same libbare-kit.so) boots cleanly with NO error on a stock Android Studio emulator (Medium_Phone_API_35, Google APIs, API 35) -- logcat shows 'Load .../libbare-kit.so using ns clns-7 from class loader ... ok' and the app reaches 'Fully drawn' with no exception. This localizes the failure to BYD's specific ROM/linker-namespace configuration (its automotive AOSP build likely has a more restrictive public-library allowlist than a stock Google image), not to bare-kit itself or to our Gradle wiring -- the identical bytes load fine under a standard Android linker config.

Per developer decision (2026-07-03): use the Android emulator as the primary dev/test target going forward; only re-test against the physical BYD unit once things are stable there. The flutter_pear_example app has been uninstalled from the BYD device for now.
notes: Diagnostic commands used: 'llvm-readelf -d libbare-kit.so | grep NEEDED' from NDK 28.2.13676358's prebuilt toolchain against the extracted debug APK. bare-kit releases checked via GitHub (v2.3.0 is latest as of 2026-07-03; no newer release exists to bump to). Related: bare-kit's own AndroidManifest declares minSdkVersion 31, two above BYD's API 29 -- possibly related to why BYD's older/more customized linker config differs from a modern stock image, but not confirmed as the root cause; a real API 31+ physical device would help isolate API-level vs ROM-specific causation.
close_reason: Developer decision (2026-07-03): BYD AUTO head unit is removed from flutter_pear's supported/target device pool entirely -- not being pursued as a bug to fix. Root cause (BYD's automotive ROM linker namespace blocking libbare-kit.so's libnativehelper.so dependency) stays recorded above for reference, but no further investigation into BYD's ROM/linker config is planned. Local development and validation now targets the Android emulator (Medium_Phone_API_35 et al., already installed) as primary; the flutter_pear_example app was uninstalled from the BYD unit. Any future physical-device testing (E1.5 two-device gate, on-device checks) should use a different physical Android device, not BYD.

title: On-device validation blocked: BYD test unit has no free storage
description:
The only physical Android device available in this environment (a BYD AUTO head unit, arm64-v8a, Android 10/API 29, connected via network adb at 192.168.0.251:5555) has only ~2GB free out of 55GB (97% used, ~40GB of which is BladeWatch dashcam video). Even an 82.5MB release-mode flutter_pear_example APK fails to install with "Requested internal only, but not enough space". Tried: installLocation=auto manifest attribute (no effect — adb install still requests internal-only), adb install -s (forcing SD-card install; not supported on this device/Android version — "Unknown option -s"). Did not attempt to free space by deleting BladeWatch footage or other data without explicit developer permission.

This blocks the on-device portions of E1.2 (app installs and launches), E1.3 (bytes flow Dart<->worklet on-device), and E1.4 (forced-error frame produces a typed Dart exception on-device) validation steps. Per developer decision (2026-07-03), these tasks proceed with build-only / mocked-channel / unit-level validation, with the actual on-device confirmation deferred to this issue.

WORK: developer frees space on the BYD unit (or supplies another arm64 Android device), then someone re-runs the on-device checks for E1.2/E1.3/E1.4's worklet boot + IPC round trip + forced-error path, ideally before/alongside E1.5's two-device gate proof (which also needs hardware the developer must supply).
acceptance_criteria: BYD unit (or a substitute arm64 device) has enough free storage to install a debug flutter_pear_example APK, OR a different physical device is supplied; on-device checks from E1.2/E1.3/E1.4 are re-run and confirmed passing on real hardware.
close_reason: Storage blocker resolved: developer freed BYD device storage (~2GB -> 40GB free, confirmed 2026-07-03). Re-ran on-device install: app installs and launches successfully on BYD (storage issue is gone), BUT hit a new, distinct, real bug -- Worklet.start() fails with a dlopen error for libnativehelper.so, specific to BYD's ROM (does not reproduce on an Android emulator). Tracked separately as flutter_pear-1y1 since it's a different root cause than what this issue was scoped to. Per developer decision (2026-07-03): use an Android emulator (Medium_Phone_API_35, already installed locally) as the primary dev/test target going forward; flutter_pear_example has been uninstalled from the BYD unit; only return to BYD hardware once things are stable on emulator.

title: E8.8: Reconcile project_plan.md (+CLAUDE.md status) with the approved design decisions
description:
CONTEXT: E8 docs. Gap found in plan-vs-beads audit (2026-07-03): the design doc's codex #14 finding requires "Reconcile the design doc WITH project_plan.md — do not let them drift", and CLAUDE.md declares project_plan.md the authoritative spec that must be kept in sync when scope changes. Scope HAS changed (office-hours + eng/DX reviews, all locked), but project_plan.md still states the pre-review plan. Today it actively misleads anyone (human or agent) told to treat it as authoritative.

DRIFT TO FIX (project_plan.md vs locked decisions in ~/.gstack/projects/andrewloable-flutter_pear/andrewloable-main-design-20260703-131146.md):
1. Section 5 M1 says "iOS + Android parity" and "CI building both platforms" — LOCKED: v0.1 is Android-only; iOS is v0.2 with its own milestone. Section 6 fresh-machine CI "builds both platforms" likewise.
2. Sections 4+5 stage releases (Corestore 0.2, Hyperbee/Hyperdrive 0.3, Autobase/pairing 0.4) — LOCKED: single v0.1 release with the full stack, no preview slices. Update the coverage-map version column + milestones to the epic structure (E1-E9).
3. Section 6 "Time-to-first-success < 15 minutes" and section 8 "<15 min" — LOCKED (DX D3/D12): champion tier, clone -> two-peer chat < 5 minutes, CI-timed + quarterly human audit.
4. Section 6 "starter repo" — moved post-v0.1, build only on observed friction (bead flutter_pear-uqs).
5. Section 4 PearCrypto topics — reflect the unsafeTopicFromString rename (E8.5) and the invites-first steer.
6. Section 7 "FFI fast path if needed" — reflect the locked bulk decision: benchmark first (E5.1), file-path import/export default, FFI stays escape hatch.
7. Autobase row — reflect prebuilt merge recipes (LWW/ordered-log/CRDT-map), generic apply = custom-worklet advanced path only.
8. Add what the plan never had: connection-state honesty contract (X8/D13), error catalog (X4), doctor tool (X5), desktop CLI peer (X7), session nonce/version handshake, native crash observation.
9. DECIDE-or-DROP while editing (log decisions): section 6 "semantic-release" (superseded by publish-on-tag in E9.7?); section 7 "track upstream releases in CI" (renovate/dependabot for Bare Kit + pear-end pins, or fold into quarterly boomerang?); section 5 M0 "Post intent on holepunchto/bare-kit discussions" (never done — do it or drop it).

ALSO: update the stale CLAUDE.md "Current status" section if it drifts from reality when this lands, and keep the two docs telling one story.

VALIDATION: a cold reader of project_plan.md alone reaches the same plan as the design doc's locked decisions; no remaining contradiction on platforms, release strategy, TTHW, bulk transport, Autobase, or naming; decide-or-drop items each have a logged decision.
acceptance_criteria: project_plan.md agrees with every locked decision (Android-first v0.1 single release, <5min TTHW, file-path bulk, Autobase recipes, unsafeTopicFromString, X8 state contract); coverage map/milestones match the E1-E9 structure; semantic-release / upstream-tracking / bare-kit-discussions items each get a logged decide-or-drop; no doc claims iOS or staged releases.
notes:
Concrete drift instance found 2026-07-03 while working E6.1: CLAUDE.md's Current status section says "Not real yet: the Bare Kit worklet itself (native still echoes)" -- but FlutterPearBarePlugin.kt already boots a REAL to.holepunch.bare.kit.Worklet (fetchBareKit in flutter_pear_bare/android/build.gradle downloads and checksum-verifies the actual v2.3.0 prebuild, not a stand-in), and suspend/resume/terminate all call real Worklet methods already. Also the packages table's row descriptions for flutter_pear/flutter_pear_bare/flutter_pear_test read as much earlier-stage than the current code (E1-E5 are substantially implemented and closed per bd). When this ticket lands, rewrite CLAUDE.md's Current status section from a fresh read of bd's closed-issue list plus the actual package contents, not from memory of the M0 scaffold state.
Another concrete drift instance found 2026-07-03 while working E6.4: packages/flutter_pear/README.md still says "Early / pre-release. M0 (worklet echo) is scaffolded; PearSwarm and friends are shaped but not yet backed by a real worklet." This is now badly stale -- E1 through E6.4 are substantially implemented and closed per bd (real Hyperswarm/Corestore/Hyperbee/Hyperdrive/Autobase/blind-pairing wrappers, real Bare Kit worklet boot, lifecycle+hot-restart correctness). When this ticket lands, rewrite this README's status line too, not just CLAUDE.md/project_plan.md.
